1. # Competitive Analysis — CDP Browser MCP Server
2. 
3. > **Date:** July 2025
4. > **Version:** v4.13.0 (current)
5. > **Purpose:** Strategic competitive analysis to guide the restructuring roadmap
6. 
7. ---
8. 
9. ## Table of Contents
10. 
11. 1. [Feature Comparison Matrix](#1-feature-comparison-matrix)
12. 2. [Our Unique Advantages](#2-our-unique-advantages)
13. 3. [Critical Gaps](#3-critical-gaps)
14. 4. [Architecture Comparison](#4-architecture-comparison)
15. 5. [Design Principles to Adopt](#5-design-principles-to-adopt)
16. 6. [Positioning Strategy](#6-positioning-strategy)
17. 
18. ---
19. 
20. ## 1. Feature Comparison Matrix
21. 
22. ### Core Capabilities
23. 
24. | Feature | CDP Browser MCP (Ours) | Playwright MCP (Microsoft) | Chrome DevTools MCP (Google) | mcp-chrome (hangwin) | Browserbase |
25. |---------|----------------------|---------------------------|-----------------------------|--------------------|-------------|
26. | **Tool count** | 11 tools, 87+ sub-actions | ~22 tools | ~29 tools | ~23 tools | 6 tools |
27. | **Architecture** | Raw CDP over WebSocket, single 254KB file | Playwright engine, modular multi-file | Puppeteer + DevTools frontend, modular | Chrome Extension + bridge | Cloud + Stagehand AI |
28. | **Language** | JavaScript (ESM) | TypeScript | TypeScript | TypeScript | TypeScript |
29. | **Dependencies** | 2 (MCP SDK + ws) | Playwright ecosystem | Puppeteer + Lighthouse + DevTools | Chrome Extension APIs | Stagehand + cloud infra |
30. | **Transport** | STDIO | STDIO, HTTP | STDIO | Streamable HTTP, STDIO | STDIO, HTTP |
31. 
32. ### Browser Management
33. 
34. | Feature | CDP Browser (Ours) | Playwright MCP | Chrome DevTools MCP | mcp-chrome | Browserbase |
35. |---------|-------------------|----------------|---------------------|------------|-------------|
36. | **Uses existing browser** | ✅ Primary mode | ⚠️ Extension mode only | ✅ Connect mode | ✅ Always | ❌ Cloud |
37. | **Manages own browser** | ❌ | ✅ Default mode | ✅ Launch mode | ❌ | ✅ Cloud |
38. | **Multi-browser (Chrome/Edge/Brave)** | ✅ Instance discovery | ❌ (Chromium/FF/WebKit) | ❌ Chrome only | ❌ Chrome only | ❌ |
39. | **Profile management** | ✅ Multi-profile listing | ✅ Per-workspace profiles | ⚠️ Single profile | ❌ | ❌ |
40. | **Auto-connect** | ❌ Manual connect needed | ✅ Auto-launch | ✅ DevToolsActivePort | ✅ Extension auto | ✅ API |
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 43m 38s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

E:\Projects\CDP Browser Automation\.research\RestructurePlan.md (lines 60-200)

<details>
<summary>141 lines</summary>

````
60. - **< 5 stale ref errors per 100 interactions**: Stable element references
61. - **0 "tabs disappeared" incidents**: Tabs NEVER auto-close unless explicitly requested
62. 
63. ### Competitive Positioning
64. 
65. ```
66.                      SIMPLE                                    POWERFUL
67.                        │                                          │
68.    Browserbase ────────┤                                          │
69.    (6 tools, NL)       │                                          │
70.                        │     Playwright MCP ──────────────────────┤
71.                        │     (22 tools, a11y)                     │
72.                        │                                          │
73.                        │          Chrome DevTools MCP ────────────┤
74.                        │          (27 tools, perf)                │
75.                        │                                          │
76.                        │               CDP Browser MCP ───────────┤
77.                        │               (50+ tools, existing browser, DevTools)
78.                        │                                          │
79.    FRESH BROWSER ──────┼──────────────────────────────── EXISTING BROWSER
80. ```
81. 
82. We are in the **top-right quadrant**: most powerful AND uses existing browser. Our challenge is making that power **accessible** — as easy to use as Playwright MCP.
83. 
84. ---
85. 
86. ## 2. Architecture Redesign
87. 
88. ### Current State: The Problem
89. 
90. ```
91. MCP Server/
92. ├── server.js          ← 254,325 bytes, 4,867 lines, EVERYTHING in one file
93. └── package.json       ← 2 dependencies (ws, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk)
94. ```
95. 
96. **Global mutable state**: 25+ `Map`/`Set` objects at module level. No encapsulation, no lifecycle management, impossible to test in isolation.
97. 
98. ### Target Architecture
99. 
100. ```
101. src/
102. ├── index.ts                      # Entry point — MCP server setup, transport init
103. ├── config.ts                     # All env vars, defaults, validation
104. ├── types.ts                      # TypeScript interfaces & type definitions
105. │
106. ├── connection/
107. │   ├── browser-discovery.ts      # Auto-detect Chrome/Edge/Brave via DevToolsActivePort
108. │   ├── cdp-client.ts             # CDP WebSocket client, message routing, callbacks
109. │   ├── cdp-session.ts            # Per-tab CDP session with domain enable/disable
110. │   ├── health-monitor.ts         # Ping/pong health checks, auto-reconnect
111. │   └── profile-manager.ts        # Profile-aware connections, instance switching
112. │
113. ├── tools/
114. │   ├── registry.ts               # Tool registration system, schema generation
115. │   ├── base-tool.ts              # Base class/interface for all tools
116. │   ├── tabs.ts                   # Tab lifecycle (list, find, new, close, activate, info)
117. │   ├── page.ts                   # Navigation, snapshot, screenshot, content, wait, PDF, dialog
118. │   ├── interact.ts               # Click, hover, type, fill, select, press, drag, scroll, upload, focus, check, tap
119. │   ├── form.ts                   # Smart form filling engine (NEW — handles all field types)
120. │   ├── execute.ts                # JS eval, script, call-on-element
121. │   ├── observe.ts                # Console, network, request body, performance, downloads, HAR
122. │   ├── emulate.ts                # Viewport, color scheme, UA, geo, CPU, timezone, locale, network, SSL
123. │   ├── storage.ts                # Cookies, localStorage, indexedDB, cache, quota
124. │   ├── intercept.ts              # HTTP request interception via Fetch domain
125. │   ├── debug.ts                  # JS debugger, breakpoints, stepping, resource overrides, DOM/event breakpoints
126. │   ├── browser.ts                # Browser/profile management, instance switching
127. │   └── cleanup.ts                # Disconnect, temp file cleanup, session end, reset
128. │
129. ├── session/
130. │   ├── session-manager.ts        # Agent session creation, lookup, TTL management
131. │   ├── tab-ownership.ts          # Tab locking (exclusive/shared), cleanup strategies
132. │   └── state-store.ts            # Global state maps (consoleLogs, networkReqs, etc.)
133. │
134. ├── snapshot/
135. │   ├── accessibility.ts          # A11y tree capture via CDP Accessibility domain
136. │   ├── element-resolver.ts       # Stable element refs via backendNodeId → DOM.resolveNode
137. │   ├── incremental.ts            # Differential snapshots (before/after diffs)
138. │   └── token-optimizer.ts        # Reduce snapshot size: depth limits, role filtering, truncation
139. │
140. ├── utils/
141. │   ├── wait.ts                   # Smart waiting: navigation, selector, text, networkidle
142. │   ├── retry.ts                  # Auto-retry with exponential backoff for stale elements
143. │   ├── temp-files.ts             # Temp file management with session-aware cleanup
144. │   ├── error-handler.ts          # Actionable error messages with fix suggestions
145. │   └── response-builder.ts       # Structured response formatting (markdown sections)
146. │
147. └── __tests__/
148.     ├── unit/
149.     │   ├── config.test.ts
150.     │   ├── cdp-client.test.ts
151.     │   ├── session-manager.test.ts
152.     │   ├── element-resolver.test.ts
153.     │   ├── token-optimizer.test.ts
154.     │   └── ...
155.     ├── integration/
156.     │   ├── tabs.test.ts
157.     │   ├── navigation.test.ts
158.     │   ├── form-filling.test.ts
159.     │   ├── interact.test.ts
160.     │   └── ...
161.     └── fixtures/
162.         ├── test-pages/            # Local HTML pages for testing
163.         └── snapshots/             # Expected snapshot outputs
164. ```
165. 
166. ### Key Architectural Decisions
167. 
168. #### 1. TypeScript Migration
169. 
170. **Why**: Type safety catches entire classes of bugs at compile time. The current server has subtle bugs from `undefined` values flowing through Maps. TypeScript also provides better IDE support and self-documentation.
171. 
172. **How**: Incremental migration. Start with `types.ts` and new modules. Convert existing code module-by-module.
173. 
174. ```json
175. // tsconfig.json
176. {
177.   "compilerOptions": {
178.     "target": "ES2022",
179.     "module": "ESNext",
180.     "moduleResolution": "node16",
181.     "outDir": "./dist",
182.     "rootDir": "./src",
183.     "strict": true,
184.     "esModuleInterop": true,
185.     "declaration": true,
186.     "sourceMap": true
187.   },
188.   "include": ["src/**/*"]
189. }
190. ```
191. 
192. #### 2. Dependency Injection via Context Object
193. 
194. Replace 25+ global Maps with a single `ServerContext` object passed to all tools:
195. 
196. ```typescript
197. interface ServerContext {
198.   config: ServerConfig;
199.   connection: CDPConnection;
200.   sessions: SessionManager;
````

</details>


---

<sub>⏱️ 43m 38s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

E:\Projects\CDP Browser Automation\.research\RestructurePlan.md (lines 200-400)

<details>
<summary>201 lines</summary>

````
200.   sessions: SessionManager;
201.   tabs: TabOwnership;
202.   state: StateStore;        // consoleLogs, networkReqs, refMaps, etc.
203.   health: HealthMonitor;
204. }
205. 
206. // Tool handler signature
207. type ToolHandler = (ctx: ServerContext, params: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<ToolResult>;
208. ```
209. 
210. **Why**: Testability (mock any dependency), encapsulation (no global leaks), lifecycle management (single place to clean up).
211. 
212. #### 3. Tool Registration System
213. 
214. ```typescript
215. // tools/registry.ts
216. interface ToolDefinition {
217.   name: string;
218.   description: string;         // Self-documenting for LLMs
219.   inputSchema: JSONSchema;
220.   annotations?: { readOnlyHint?: boolean; destructiveHint?: boolean };
221.   handler: ToolHandler;
222. }
223. 
224. class ToolRegistry {
225.   private tools = new Map<string, ToolDefinition>();
226. 
227.   register(tool: ToolDefinition): void { ... }
228.   list(): ToolDefinition[] { ... }
229.   dispatch(name: string, args: unknown): Promise<ToolResult> { ... }
230. }
231. ```
232. 
233. #### 4. Event-Driven CDP Client
234. 
235. Replace raw WebSocket message handling with typed event emitter:
236. 
237. ```typescript
238. class CDPClient extends EventEmitter {
239.   async send(method: string, params?: object, sessionId?: string): Promise<unknown>;
240.   on(event: 'Target.targetCreated', handler: (params: TargetCreatedParams) => void): this;
241.   on(event: 'Network.requestWillBeSent', handler: (params: RequestParams) => void): this;
242.   // ... typed for every CDP event we use
243. }
244. ```
245. 
246. #### 5. Auto-Connect on First Tool Call
247. 
248. ```typescript
249. // index.ts — in tool dispatch
250. async function handleToolCall(name: string, args: unknown): Promise<ToolResult> {
251.   // Auto-connect if not connected — transparent to the agent
252.   if (!ctx.connection.isConnected()) {
253.     await ctx.connection.autoConnect();  // Discovers browser, connects, no user action needed
254.   }
255.   return registry.dispatch(name, args);
256. }
257. ```
258. 
259. ---
260. 
261. ## 3. Critical Fixes — Phase 1 (Week 1–2)
262. 
263. These are the top 10 user-identified issues that block core workflows. Fix them FIRST, even before the full architecture migration.
264. 
265. ### Fix 1: Auto-Connect on Startup
266. 
267. **Problem**: Agents start fresh and immediately try `tabs.new()` without connecting. Server fails silently or returns "no target found". No tool says "you need to connect first".
268. 
269. **Solution**: Auto-connect on the first tool call. Discover the browser automatically using `DevToolsActivePort` file scanning (already implemented in `discoverChromeInstances()`).
270. 
271. ```typescript
272. // Before EVERY tool call:
273. async function ensureConnected(ctx: ServerContext): Promise<void> {
274.   if (ctx.connection.isConnected()) return;
275. 
276.   // Try auto-connect: scan DevToolsActivePort files
277.   const instances = discoverChromeInstances();
278.   if (instances.length === 0) {
279.     throw new ActionableError(
280.       'No browser found with remote debugging enabled.',
281.       'Enable remote debugging: chrome://flags → #enable-remote-debugging → Enabled → Relaunch Chrome'
282.     );
283.   }
284. 
285.   // Prefer CDP_PROFILE env var match, then Chrome, then Edge, then Brave
286.   const target = instances.find(i => i.name === process.env.CDP_PROFILE)
287.     || instances.find(i => i.name === 'Chrome')
288.     || instances[0];
289. 
290.   await ctx.connection.connect(target.wsUrl);
291. }
292. ```
293. 
294. **Files to change**: `connection/cdp-client.ts`, `index.ts`
295. **Tests**: Unit test for auto-discovery, integration test for first-call auto-connect
296. 
297. ---
298. 
299. ### Fix 2: Default `cleanupStrategy` to "detach"
300. 
301. **Problem**: Default `cleanupStrategy: "close"` kills ALL tabs when agent session ends. Users lose 35+ tabs of important work.
302. 
303. **Solution**: Change default from `"close"` to `"detach"`. Tabs stay open when session expires. Add a `CDP_CLEANUP_STRATEGY` env var for override.
304. 
305. ```typescript
306. // config.ts
307. const DEFAULT_CLEANUP_STRATEGY = process.env.CDP_CLEANUP_STRATEGY || 'detach';  // was 'close'
308. ```
309. 
310. **Also**: Add clear warning in tool descriptions:
311. ```
312. cleanupStrategy: Tab cleanup when session expires.
313.   "detach" (default) — tabs stay open after session ends
314.   "close" — tabs are closed when session expires (USE WITH CAUTION)
315.   "none" — no cleanup at all
316. ```
317. 
318. **Files to change**: `config.ts`, all tool schemas that reference cleanupStrategy
319. **Tests**: Verify tabs persist after session TTL expiry with default config
320. 
321. ---
322. 
323. ### Fix 3: Fix Fresh Tab `about:blank` Issue
324. 
325. **Problem**: `tabs.new({ url: "https://..." })` frequently opens `about:blank`. Auth cookies don't propagate to new tabs properly.
326. 
327. **Root Cause**: CDP `Target.createTarget` creates a new tab, but navigation happens before the tab's CDP session is fully attached. The `goto` races against session setup.
328. 
329. **Solution**: Two-phase tab creation with explicit wait:
330. 
331. ```typescript
332. async function createTab(url: string, ctx: ServerContext): Promise<TabInfo> {
333.   // 1. Create tab without URL (avoids race condition)
334.   const { targetId } = await ctx.connection.send('Target.createTarget', {
335.     url: 'about:blank',
336.     newWindow: false,
337.     // If profile specified, use browserContextId
338.   });
339. 
340.   // 2. Attach to the new tab's CDP session
341.   const session = await ctx.connection.attachToTarget(targetId);
342. 
343.   // 3. Enable required domains FIRST
344.   await session.enableDomains(['Page', 'Runtime', 'Network', 'DOM']);
345. 
346.   // 4. NOW navigate — session is ready, cookies will propagate
347.   await session.send('Page.navigate', { url });
348. 
349.   // 5. Wait for load with smart timeout
350.   await waitForNavigation(session, { timeout: ctx.config.timeout });
351. 
352.   return { targetId, session, url };
353. }
354. ```
355. 
356. **Files to change**: `tools/tabs.ts`
357. **Tests**: Integration test creating 10 tabs with authenticated URLs, verify none land on `about:blank`
358. 
359. ---
360. 
361. ### Fix 4: Stable Element Refs
362. 
363. **Problem**: Between taking a snapshot and clicking an element, refs change. "Element ref=284 not found" happens constantly. Agents fall back to JavaScript DOM queries.
364. 
365. **Current Implementation**: Uses `backendNodeId` (Chrome's internal DOM ID) which IS stable across snapshots. The issue is the `refMap` (uid → backendNodeId) gets cleared/rebuilt on every new snapshot, invalidating previously-seen uids.
366. 
367. **Solution**: Persist refMaps across snapshots within the same page:
368. 
369. ```typescript
370. class ElementResolver {
371.   // Cumulative map — never cleared, only grows. Cleared on navigation.
372.   private globalRefMap = new Map<number, number>();  // uid → backendNodeId
373.   private nextUid = 1;
374. 
375.   // On new snapshot: assign uids to new backendNodeIds, reuse existing uids for known nodes
376.   assignRefs(nodes: AXNode[]): void {
377.     const backendIdToUid = new Map<number, number>();
378.     // Build reverse lookup from existing refs
379.     for (const [uid, bid] of this.globalRefMap) {
380.       backendIdToUid.set(bid, uid);
381.     }
382. 
383.     for (const node of nodes) {
384.       if (backendIdToUid.has(node.backendNodeId)) {
385.         node.uid = backendIdToUid.get(node.backendNodeId)!;  // Reuse existing uid
386.       } else {
387.         node.uid = this.nextUid++;                           // New uid
388.         this.globalRefMap.set(node.uid, node.backendNodeId);
389.         backendIdToUid.set(node.backendNodeId, node.uid);
390.       }
391.     }
392.   }
393. 
394.   // On page navigation — reset everything
395.   onNavigation(): void {
396.     this.globalRefMap.clear();
397.     this.nextUid = 1;
398.   }
399. }
400. ```
````

</details>

````
1. # Skill: ultrawork
2. 
3. > Full task lifecycle: plan -> implement -> verify -> review -> commit.
4. > The "do everything right" workflow.
5. > Inspired by: oh-my-codex ($ultrawork), oh-my-claudecode (ultrawork)
6. 
7. ## When to Trigger
8. 
9. - User says "ultrawork", "do this properly", "full workflow"
10. - For any significant task that warrants the full treatment
11. 
12. ## Workflow
13. 
14. ```
15. 1. PLAN        → Create structured plan (follow skills/plan.md workflow, or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it)
16. 2. IMPLEMENT   → Execute the plan, file by file
17. 3. VERIFY      → Run tests, linters, type-checkers (follow skills/verify.md workflow, or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it)
18. 4. REVIEW      → Self-review changes (follow skills/review.md workflow, or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it)
19. 5. DOCUMENT    → Update relevant docs (follow skills/doc-maintainer.md workflow, or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it)
20. 6. COMMIT      → Suggest committing in logical batches with good messages
21. ```
22. 
23. ### Step 1 — Plan
24. Follow the workflow in skills/plan.md (or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it). Create a structured plan, self-critique it,
25. and share with the user for approval (unless the user asked for autonomous execution).
26. 
27. ### Step 2 — Implement
28. Execute the plan in order. For each file:
29. - Make the change
30. - Verify it's consistent with the plan
31. - If a surprise comes up, pause and revise the plan
32. 
33. ### Step 3 — Verify
34. Run the project's test/lint/build commands:
35. - Test suite (project's test command)
36. - Linter (project's lint command)
37. - Build (project's build command)
38. - Type checking if available
39. - If nothing exists, do a manual sanity check
40. 
41. ### Step 4 — Review
42. Follow the workflow in skills/review.md (or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it) on your own changes.
43. Fix any issues found before proceeding.
44. 
45. ### Step 5 — Document
46. Follow the workflow in skills/doc-maintainer.md (or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it) to update any docs affected by the changes.
47. This includes README, API docs, changelogs, progress trackers.
48. 
49. ### Step 6 — Commit
50. Suggest committing. Only commit if user approves or repo workflow permits autonomous commits.
51. - Group related changes together
52. - Write descriptive commit messages (not "fix stuff")
53. - Use conventional commits format: feat/fix/refactor/docs/chore
54. 
55. ## Rules
56. 
57. - If ANY step fails, stop and report. Don't push through broken code.
58. - The user can skip steps explicitly ("skip the review")
59. - For trivial tasks, suggest using plan+implement only (skip verify/review/doc)
60. - Always report what was done at the end
61. - When user invokes ultrawork, do NOT separately invoke plan — ultrawork includes planning. Plan skill is for standalone planning only.
62.
````

````
400. ```
401. 
402. **Key insight**: The `backendNodeId` from CDP's Accessibility domain IS stable. The problem is our uid assignment layer. By mapping backendNodeId → uid consistently, refs survive across multiple snapshot calls on the same page.
403. 
404. **Files to change**: `snapshot/element-resolver.ts`
405. **Tests**: Take snapshot, modify DOM, take new snapshot — verify same elements get same uids
406. 
407. ---
408. 
409. ### Fix 5: Smart Form Filling for React Comboboxes
410. 
411. **Problem**: Greenhouse, Lever, and LinkedIn use React comboboxes. `fill()` types text but dropdown doesn't select. Currently requires 5 tool calls for ONE dropdown: type → wait → snapshot → find option → click option.
412. 
413. **Solution**: New `form.smartFill()` action that handles all field types:
414. 
415. ```typescript
416. async function smartFillCombobox(session: CDPSession, uid: number, value: string): Promise<void> {
417.   const node = await resolveElement(session, uid);
418. 
419.   // 1. Focus the combobox
420.   await session.send('DOM.focus', { backendNodeId: node.backendNodeId });
421. 
422.   // 2. Clear existing value
423.   await session.send('Input.dispatchKeyEvent', { type: 'keyDown', key: 'a', modifiers: 2 }); // Ctrl+A
424.   await session.send('Input.dispatchKeyEvent', { type: 'keyDown', key: 'Backspace' });
425. 
426.   // 3. Type the value character by character (triggers React's onChange)
427.   for (const char of value) {
428.     await session.send('Input.dispatchKeyEvent', { type: 'keyDown', key: char });
429.     await session.send('Input.dispatchKeyEvent', { type: 'char', text: char });
430.     await session.send('Input.dispatchKeyEvent', { type: 'keyUp', key: char });
431.     await sleep(50); // Allow React to process each keystroke
432.   }
433. 
434.   // 4. Wait for dropdown options to appear (poll accessibility tree)
435.   const options = await waitForOptions(session, value, { timeout: 3000 });
436. 
437.   // 5. Find best match and click it
438.   if (options.length > 0) {
439.     const bestMatch = findBestMatch(options, value);
440.     await clickElement(session, bestMatch.backendNodeId);
441.   }
442. }
443. ```
444. 
445. **Also add `interact.fill` action `fields` parameter for batch form filling**:
446. 
447. ```typescript
448. // New: fill multiple fields in one call
449. {
450.   action: "fill",
451.   tabId: "...",
452.   fields: [
453.     { uid: 5, value: "John Doe", type: "text" },
454.     { uid: 8, value: "Bachelor's Degree", type: "combobox" },
455.     { uid: 12, value: "true", type: "checkbox" },
456.     { uid: 15, value: "2025-01-15", type: "date" }
457.   ]
458. }
459. ```
460. 
461. **Files to change**: `tools/form.ts` (new), `tools/interact.ts` (add `fields` param)
462. **Tests**: Test against local HTML pages with React-style comboboxes, native `<select>`, checkboxes, date inputs
463. 
464. ---
465. 
466. ### Fix 6: Increase Default Timeout for SPAs
467. 
468. **Problem**: D365, SharePoint, enterprise apps take 60–90+ seconds to load. `CDP_TIMEOUT` of 30s causes constant failures. Multiple retry loops burn agent context.
469. 
470. **Solution**: Tiered timeout system:
471. 
472. ```typescript
473. // config.ts
474. const config = {
475.   actionTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_ACTION_TIMEOUT) || 10_000,      // Click, type, etc.
476.   navigationTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT) || 60_000, // Page.navigate
477.   snapshotTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_SNAPSHOT_TIMEOUT) || 15_000,    // A11y tree capture
478.   globalTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_TIMEOUT) || 60_000,              // Fallback (was 30000)
479. };
480. ```
481. 
482. **Also**: The `page.goto` action should accept a `timeout` parameter to override per-call:
483. 
484. ```typescript
485. // page.goto with custom timeout
486. { action: "goto", tabId: "...", url: "https://d365.example.com", timeout: 120000 }
487. ```
488. 
489. **Files to change**: `config.ts`, `tools/page.ts`
490. **Tests**: Verify navigation to slow page works with 60s default
491. 
492. ---
493. 
494. ### Fix 7: Reduce Snapshot Size with Token Optimization
495. 
496. **Problem**: Gmail, D365, LinkedIn snapshots are 30–40KB. Agents can't process them. Snapshots get saved to temp files, requiring extra grep round-trips.
497. 
498. **Solution**: Multi-strategy token optimization:
499. 
500. ```typescript
501. class TokenOptimizer {
502.   // Strategy 1: Depth limiting — only expand nodes up to N levels deep
503.   static limitDepth(tree: AXNode, maxDepth: number = 5): AXNode { ... }
504. 
505.   // Strategy 2: Role filtering — skip decorative/structural roles
506.   static filterRoles(tree: AXNode): AXNode {
507.     const SKIP_ROLES = new Set([
508.       'generic', 'none', 'presentation', 'separator',
509.       'LineBreak', 'InlineTextBox', 'StaticText'  // Merge into parent
510.     ]);
511.     return filterTree(tree, node => !SKIP_ROLES.has(node.role));
512.   }
513. 
514.   // Strategy 3: Text truncation — cap long text content at 100 chars
515.   static truncateText(tree: AXNode, maxLen: number = 100): AXNode { ... }
516. 
517.   // Strategy 4: Collapse empty containers
518.   static collapseEmpty(tree: AXNode): AXNode {
519.     // Remove nodes with no name, no children, and a generic role
520.     return filterTree(tree, node => node.name || node.children?.length || isInteractive(node));
521.   }
522. 
523.   // Combined pipeline
524.   static optimize(tree: AXNode, budget: number = 20_000): string {
525.     let result = tree;
526.     result = this.filterRoles(result);
527.     result = this.collapseEmpty(result);
528.     result = this.truncateText(result);
529. 
530.     const serialized = serialize(result);
531.     if (serialized.length > budget) {
532.       result = this.limitDepth(result, 4);
533.     }
534.     return serialize(result);
535.   }
536. }
537. ```
538. 
539. **Target**: Reduce average snapshot from 34KB to < 15KB without losing interactive elements.
540. 
541. **Files to change**: `snapshot/token-optimizer.ts` (new)
542. **Tests**: Optimize snapshots of Gmail, LinkedIn, D365 pages — verify all interactive elements are preserved
543. 
544. ---
545. 
546. ### Fix 8: Auto-Reconnect on Browser Disconnect
547. 
548. **Problem**: When browser restarts or debugging port changes, server silently fails. No reconnection attempt, no health check warning.
549. 
550. **Solution**: Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff:
551. 
552. ```typescript
553. class HealthMonitor {
554.   private reconnectAttempts = 0;
555.   private maxReconnectAttempts = 5;
556. 
557.   async onDisconnect(): Promise<void> {
558.     // Clear all state (already done)
559.     this.clearState();
560. 
561.     // Try to reconnect with backoff
562.     while (this.reconnectAttempts < this.maxReconnectAttempts) {
563.       this.reconnectAttempts++;
564.       const delay = Math.min(1000 * Math.pow(2, this.reconnectAttempts - 1), 10000);
565.       await sleep(delay);
566. 
567.       try {
568.         await this.connection.autoConnect();
569.         this.reconnectAttempts = 0;
570.         return;
571.       } catch {
572.         // Will retry
573.       }
574.     }
575. 
576.     // After max attempts, give up but provide clear error
577.     this.status = 'disconnected';
578.   }
579. }
580. ```
581. 
582. **Also**: Return connection status in tool error messages:
583. 
584. ```
585. Error: Browser is disconnected. Attempted 5 reconnections, all failed.
586. Fix: Ensure Chrome is running with remote debugging enabled.
587.   chrome://flags → #enable-remote-debugging → Enabled → Relaunch Chrome
588. Status: Last connected 2 minutes ago. Browser may have been closed or restarted.
589. ```
590. 
591. **Files to change**: `connection/health-monitor.ts`, `utils/error-handler.ts`
592. **Tests**: Simulate WebSocket close, verify auto-reconnect within 5 seconds
593. 
594. ---
595. 
596. ### Fix 9: Clear Actionable Error Messages
597. 
598. **Problem**: Errors like "No target found", "Session not found", "Cannot read properties of undefined" give agents no clue what to do.
599. 
600. **Solution**: Error wrapping with fix suggestions:
````

</details>

<details>

````
600. **Solution**: Error wrapping with fix suggestions:
601. 
602. ```typescript
603. class ActionableError extends Error {
604.   constructor(
605.     message: string,
606.     public readonly fix: string,
607.     public readonly context?: Record<string, unknown>
608.   ) {
609.     super(message);
610.   }
611. 
612.   toToolResult(): ToolResult {
613.     return {
614.       content: [{
615.         type: 'text',
616.         text: [
617.           `### Error`,
618.           this.message,
619.           ``,
620.           `### How to fix`,
621.           this.fix,
622.           this.context ? `\n### Context\n${JSON.stringify(this.context, null, 2)}` : ''
623.         ].join('\n')
624.       }],
625.       isError: true
626.     };
627.   }
628. }
629. 
630. // Error catalog
631. const ERRORS = {
632.   NO_BROWSER: (port: number) => new ActionableError(
633.     `Cannot connect to browser on port ${port}.`,
634.     `Enable remote debugging:\n  Chrome: chrome://flags → #enable-remote-debugging → Enabled → Relaunch\n  Edge: edge://flags → same setting\n  Brave: brave://flags → same setting`
635.   ),
636. 
637.   STALE_REF: (uid: number) => new ActionableError(
638.     `Element uid=${uid} not found — the page may have changed since the last snapshot.`,
639.     `Take a fresh snapshot with page.snapshot(), then use the new uid from the snapshot.`
640.   ),
641. 
642.   TAB_NOT_FOUND: (tabId: string) => new ActionableError(
643.     `Tab "${tabId}" not found. It may have been closed or its session expired.`,
644.     `Use tabs.list({ showAll: true }) to see all available tabs, then use the correct tabId.`
645.   ),
646. 
647.   SESSION_EXPIRED: (ttl: number) => new ActionableError(
648.     `Session expired after ${ttl / 1000}s of inactivity.`,
649.     `Sessions auto-renew on every tool call. If you need longer TTL, set CDP_SESSION_TTL env var (default: 300000ms = 5 min).`
650.   ),
651. 
652.   NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT: (url: string, timeout: number) => new ActionableError(
653.     `Navigation to "${url}" timed out after ${timeout / 1000}s.`,
654.     `The page may still be loading. Try:\n  1. page.wait({ timeout: 10000 }) then page.snapshot()\n  2. Increase timeout: page.goto({ url, timeout: 120000 })\n  3. Use waitUntil: "domcontentloaded" for faster (partial) load`
655.   ),
656. 
657.   COMBOBOX_FAILED: (value: string) => new ActionableError(
658.     `Could not select "${value}" from dropdown. No matching option found.`,
659.     `Try:\n  1. Type the value first: interact.type({ uid, text: "${value}" })\n  2. Wait for dropdown: page.wait({ timeout: 2000 })\n  3. Take snapshot to see options: page.snapshot()\n  4. Click the matching option by its uid`
660.   ),
661. };
662. ```
663. 
664. **Files to change**: `utils/error-handler.ts` (new), wrap all existing error throws
665. **Tests**: Trigger each error scenario, verify fix text appears in response
666. 
667. ---
668. 
669. ### Fix 10: Console Error Filtering
670. 
671. **Problem**: Every tool response includes `### Console Errors` with Chrome extension errors (e.g., "TypeError: Failed to set innerHTML" from a Chrome extension). This adds noise and wastes context tokens.
672. 
673. **Solution**: Filter out extension-originating console errors by default:
674. 
675. ```typescript
676. function filterConsoleErrors(logs: ConsoleLog[]): ConsoleLog[] {
677.   const EXTENSION_PATTERNS = [
678.     /chrome-extension:\/\//,
679.     /moz-extension:\/\//,
680.     /^Uncaught.*extension/i,
681.     /^Failed to load resource.*chrome-extension/,
682.     /^Denying load of chrome-extension/,
683.   ];
684. 
685.   return logs.filter(log => {
686.     // Keep non-error messages as-is
687.     if (log.level !== 'error') return true;
688.     // Filter out extension errors
689.     return !EXTENSION_PATTERNS.some(pat => pat.test(log.text) || pat.test(log.url || ''));
690.   });
691. }
692. ```
693. 
694. **Also**: Add `console` parameter to snapshot tool to control inclusion:
695. 
696. ```typescript
697. // page.snapshot({ console: "errors" })     — only page errors (default)
698. // page.snapshot({ console: "all" })         — everything including extensions
699. // page.snapshot({ console: "none" })        — suppress console section entirely
700. ```
701. 
702. **Files to change**: `tools/observe.ts`, `tools/page.ts`
703. **Tests**: Inject extension-style console error, verify it's filtered from snapshot response
704. 
705. ---
706. 
707. ## 4. Architecture Migration — Phase 2 (Week 3–4)
708. 
709. ### Migration Strategy: Incremental Extraction
710. 
711. The key constraint is **zero downtime** — the server must work at every commit. We extract modules one at a time from `server.js`, with each extraction being a self-contained PR.
712. 
713. ### Step-by-Step Migration Order
714. 
715. ```
716. Week 3:
717.   Day 1-2: Project setup (TypeScript, build pipeline, package.json updates)
718.   Day 3:   Extract types.ts + config.ts
719.   Day 4:   Extract connection/ (cdp-client.ts, browser-discovery.ts)
720.   Day 5:   Extract session/ (session-manager.ts, tab-ownership.ts, state-store.ts)
721. 
722. Week 4:
723.   Day 1:   Extract snapshot/ (accessibility.ts, element-resolver.ts)
724.   Day 2:   Extract utils/ (wait.ts, retry.ts, temp-files.ts, error-handler.ts)
725.   Day 3-4: Extract tools/ (one tool module per half-day)
726.   Day 5:   Extract index.ts (entry point), remove server.js, verify everything works
727. ```
728. 
729. ### Project Setup
730. 
731. ```json
732. // package.json changes
733. {
734.   "name": "cdp-mcp-server",
735.   "version": "5.0.0",
736.   "type": "module",
737.   "main": "dist/index.js",
738.   "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
739.   "scripts": {
740.     "build": "tsc",
741.     "start": "node dist/index.js",
742.     "dev": "tsc --watch",
743.     "test": "vitest",
744.     "test:integration": "vitest run --config vitest.integration.config.ts",
745.     "lint": "eslint src/",
746.     "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
747.   },
748.   "devDependencies": {
749.     "typescript": "^5.5.0",
750.     "vitest": "^2.0.0",
751.     "@types/ws": "^8.0.0",
752.     "eslint": "^9.0.0",
753.     "@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin": "^8.0.0"
754.   },
755.   "dependencies": {
756.     "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.0.0",
757.     "ws": "^8.0.0"
758.   }
759. }
760. ```
761. 
762. ### Module Extraction Pattern
763. 
764. For each module extraction:
765. 
766. 1. **Create the new file** with the extracted code
767. 2. **Add TypeScript types** to all function signatures
768. 3. **Replace global state** with injected context parameters
769. 4. **Update imports** in the remaining `server.js`
770. 5. **Run the test suite** to verify no regressions
771. 6. **Commit with clear message**: `refactor: extract {module} from server.js`
772. 
773. ### Example: Extracting `config.ts`
774. 
775. ```typescript
776. // src/config.ts
777. export interface ServerConfig {
778.   cdpHost: string;
779.   cdpPort: number;
780.   actionTimeout: number;
781.   navigationTimeout: number;
782.   snapshotTimeout: number;
783.   sessionTTL: number;
784.   debuggerTimeout: number;
785.   cleanupStrategy: 'close' | 'detach' | 'none';
786.   tempDir: string;
787.   maxTempFiles: number;
788.   maxTempAgeMs: number;
789.   maxInlineLen: number;
790. }
791. 
792. export function loadConfig(): ServerConfig {
793.   return {
794.     cdpHost: process.env.CDP_HOST || '127.0.0.1',
795.     cdpPort: parseInt(process.env.CDP_PORT || '9222'),
796.     actionTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_ACTION_TIMEOUT || '10000'),
797.     navigationTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT || '60000'),
798.     snapshotTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_SNAPSHOT_TIMEOUT || '15000'),
799.     sessionTTL: parseInt(process.env.CDP_SESSION_TTL || '300000'),
800.     debuggerTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_DEBUGGER_TIMEOUT || '30000'),
````

</details>

1. #!/usr/bin/env node
2. 
3. /**
4.  * CDP Browser Automation — MCP Server  v4.13.0
5.  *
6.  * 11 tools with 87+ sub-actions for full browser automation.
7.  * Features: stable element refs (backendNodeId), auto-waiting, incremental snapshots,
8.  * per-agent session isolation with tab locking, framework-aware inputs, modal guards,
9.  * human-like interaction mode, Chrome instance/profile management, JavaScript debugger
10.  * with breakpoints/stepping/call-stack inspection, resource overrides, DOM/event breakpoints,
11.  * auto-console-error reporting, connection health monitoring, download tracking,
12.  * cross-origin (OOP) iframe support via Target.setAutoAttach.
13.  *
14.  * Tools:
15.  *   tabs      — Tab lifecycle (list, find, new, close, activate, info)
16.  *   page      — Navigation, snapshot, screenshot, content, set_content, add_style, wait, PDF, dialog, inject
17.  *   interact  — Click, hover, type, fill, select, press, drag, scroll, upload, focus, check, tap
18.  *   execute   — JS eval, script, call-on-element
19.  *   observe   — Console, network, request body, performance metrics, downloads
20.  *   emulate   — Viewport, color, UA, geo, CPU, timezone, locale, vision, network, SSL, etc.
21.  *   storage   — Cookies, localStorage, indexedDB, cache, quota
22.  *   intercept — HTTP request interception, mocking, blocking via Fetch domain
23.  *   cleanup   — Disconnect sessions, clean temp files, status, list_sessions, session, reset
24.  *   browser   — Browser instance discovery (Chrome, Edge, Brave), profile listing, connection switching
25.  *   debug     — JS debugger (breakpoints, stepping, call stack), resource overrides, DOM/event breakpoints
26.  *
27.  * Setup:  chrome://flags/#enable-remote-debugging → Enabled → Relaunch (or edge://flags for Edge)
28.  *
29.  * Env vars:
30.  *   CDP_PORT              Browser debugging port            (default: 9222)
31.  *   CDP_HOST              Browser debugging host            (default: 127.0.0.1)
32.  *   CDP_TIMEOUT           Command timeout in ms             (default: 30000)
33.  *   CDP_SESSION_TTL       Agent session TTL in ms           (default: 300000)
34.  *   CDP_USER_DATA         Chrome User Data directory path
35.  *   CDP_PROFILE           Auto-connect to Chrome instance by name or path
36.  *   CDP_DEBUGGER_TIMEOUT  Debugger auto-resume timeout ms   (default: 30000)
37.  */
38.

Profile `{ name: string, email?: string, directory: string }`\n- `AXNode` — Accessibility tree node `{ role: string, name: string, uid?: number, backendNodeId: number, children?: AXNode[], properties?: Record<string, unknown> }`\n- `CleanupStrategy` — `'close' | 'detach' | 'none'`\n- `ToolHandler` — `(ctx: ServerContext, params: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<ToolResult>`\n- (process.env.CDP_CLEANUP_STRATEGY as CleanupStrategy) || 'detach',  // was 'close'\n    \n    maxInlineLen: 60_000,\n    tempDir: process.env.CDP_TEMP_DIR || '',  // resolved at runtime\n    maxTempFiles: 50,\n    maxTempAgeMs: 30 * 60 * 1000,\n    \n    // Auto-connect\n    autoConnectProfile: process.env.CDP_PROFILE || '',\n  };\n}\n```\n\nMake sure both files compile: run `cd \"E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server\" && npx tsc --noEmit` to verify.\n\nWhen done, update the SQL todo:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id = 'extract-config'\n```"
}
````

<sub>⏱️ 47m 19s</sub>

### ✅ `task`

**Create error handler module**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Create error handler module",
  "mode": "background",
  "name": "extract-errors",
  "prompt": "Create the error handler module for the CDP Browser MCP Server.\n\nWorking directory: `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server`\nBranch: `dev/v5-restructure` (already checked out)\n\n## Task: Create `src/utils/error-handler.ts`\n\nThis implements Fix 9 from the restructure plan — actionable error messages that tell agents HOW to fix the problem.\n\nCreate the file with:\n\n### 1. ActionableError class\n```typescript\nexport class ActionableError extends Error {\n  public readonly fix: string;\n  public readonly context?: Record<string, unknown>;\n\n  constructor(message: string, fix: string, context?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    super(message);\n    this.name = 'ActionableError';\n    this.fix = fix;\n    this.context = context;\n  }\n\n  toToolResult(): { content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }>; isError: true } {\n    const parts = [`### Error\\n${this.message}`, `\\n### How to fix\\n${this.fix}`];\n    if (this.context) {\n      parts.push(`\\n### Context\\n${JSON.stringify(this.context, null, 2)}`);\n    }\n    return {\n      content: [{ type: 'text', text: parts.join('\\n') }],\n      isError: true,\n    };\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 2. Error Catalog — Named error factory functions\nCreate factory functions for EVERY common error the server can produce. Read server.js to find all error patterns (grep for \"throw\", \"Error(\", \"isError: true\"). Include at minimum:\n\n- `noBrowser(port)` — No browser found with remote debugging on this port\n- `staleRef(uid)` — Element uid not found, page may have changed\n- `tabNotFound(tabId)` — Tab closed or session expired\n- `sessionExpired(ttl)` — Session expired after inactivity\n- `navigationTimeout(url, timeout)` — Page didn't load in time\n- `comboboxFailed(value)` — Couldn't select value from dropdown\n- `notConnected()` — Server not connected to any browser\n- `targetDetached(tabId)` — CDP target was detached (tab crashed or navigated away)\n- `frameNotFound(frameId)` — Frame not found in page\n- `elementNotInteractable(uid, reason)` — Element exists but can't be interacted with\n- `fileNotFound(path)` — File path for upload doesn't exist\n- `cdpError(method, errorMsg)` — Raw CDP protocol error wrapper\n- `dialogBlocking(dialogType)` — A dialog is blocking, handle it first\n- `debuggerPaused()` — Debugger is paused, resume before other operations\n\n### 3. Helper: wrapError function\n```typescript\nexport function wrapError(error: unknown): ActionableError {\n  if (error instanceof ActionableError) return error;\n  const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);\n  // Try to match known patterns and return appropriate ActionableError\n  if (msg.includes('No target with given id')) return Errors.targetDetached('unknown');\n  if (msg.includes('not found')) return Errors.staleRef(0);\n  // ... more pattern matching\n  return new ActionableError(msg, 'Check the error message for details. Try taking a fresh snapshot.');\n}\n```\n\nVerify it compiles: `cd \"E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server\" && npx tsc --noEmit`\n\nWhen done:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id = 'extract-errors'\n```"
}
````

</details>

{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Extract connection modules",

  "name": "extract-connection",
  "prompt": "Extract connection-related modules from the CDP Browser MCP Server monolith.\n\nWorking directory: `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server`\nBranch: `dev/v5-restructure` (already checked out)\n\nRead server.js to understand the current connection implementation. Search for:\n- WebSocket connection code\n- `discoverChromeInstances` function\n- Browser detection / DevToolsActivePort scanning\n- Reconnection logic\n- The `cdpSend` function and CDP message routing\n\n## Task: Create 3 files\n\n### 1. `src/connection/cdp-client.ts`\nA typed CDP WebSocket client. Extract from server.js:\n- WebSocket connection/disconnection\n- `cdpSend(method, params, sessionId)` → typed Promise-based message sending\n- Message ID tracking and callback resolution\n- Event listener registration (CDP events like Target.targetCreated, Network.requestWillBeSent)\n- Connection state management (connected/disconnected/connecting)\n- Typed EventEmitter for CDP events\n\nKey interface:\n```typescript\nimport { EventEmitter } from 'events';\nimport WebSocket from 'ws';\n\nexport class CDPClient extends EventEmitter {\n  private ws: WebSocket | null = null;\n  private messageId = 0;\n  private callbacks = new Map<number, { resolve: Function; reject: Function; timer: NodeJS.Timeout }>();\n  \n  get isConnected(): boolean;\n  async connect(wsUrl: string): Promise<void>;\n  disconnect(): void;\n  async send(method: string, params?: object, sessionId?: string): Promise<unknown>;\n  // Event routing for CDP domain events\n}\n```\n\n### 2. `src/connection/browser-discovery.ts`\nExtract browser auto-detection. Find in server.js the `discoverChromeInstances` function that scans for Chrome/Edge/Brave debugging ports via DevToolsActivePort files on the filesystem.\n\n```typescript\nexport interface BrowserInstance {\n  name: string;           // 'Chrome' | 'Edge' | 'Brave'\n  port: number;\n  wsUrl: string;\n  userDataDir: string;\n  pid?: number;\n}\n\nexport function discoverBrowserInstances(): BrowserInstance[];\nexport function findBestInstance(instances: BrowserInstance[], preferredProfile?: string): BrowserInstance | null;\n```\n\n### 3. `src/connection/health-monitor.ts`\nCreate the auto-reconnect system (Fix 1 + Fix 8):\n\n```typescript\nexport class HealthMonitor {\n  private reconnectAttempts = 0;\n  private maxAttempts = 5;\n  private client: CDPClient;\n  \n  constructor(client: CDPClient);\n  \n  // Called when WebSocket closes unexpectedly\n  async onDisconnect(): Promise<boolean>;  // returns true if reconnected\n  \n  // Auto-connect: find browser, connect. Called on first tool call.\n  async autoConnect(): Promise<void>;\n  \n  // Reset reconnect counter on successful connection\n  onConnected(): void;\n}\n```\n\nVerify compilation: `cd \"E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server\" && npx tsc --noEmit`\n\nNote: These files may import from `../types.js` and `../config.js` — if those don't exist yet, create minimal stub versions or use inline types. The other agents are creating those files in parallel.\n\nWhen done:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id = 'extract-connection'\n```"
}

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Extract snapshot modules",
  "mode": "background",
  "name": "extract-snapshot",
  "prompt": "Extract snapshot-related modules from the CDP Browser MCP Server monolith.\n\nWorking directory: `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server`\nBranch: `dev/v5-restructure` (already checked out)\n\nRead server.js to understand the current snapshot implementation. Search for:\n- `Accessibility.getFullAXTree` calls\n- `backendNodeId` handling and the ref/uid mapping system\n- How snapshots are serialized to text\n- The `resolveRef` / `resolveBackend` functions\n\n## Task: Create 3 files\n\n### 1. `src/snapshot/accessibility.ts`\nExtract the accessibility tree capture logic:\n\n```typescript\nexport interface AXNode {\n  role: string;\n  name: string;\n  uid?: number;\n  backendNodeId: number;\n  value?: string;\n  description?: string;\n  properties?: Record<string, unknown>;\n  children?: AXNode[];\n  frameId?: string;\n  // State properties\n  focused?: boolean;\n  selected?: boolean;\n  checked?: boolean | 'mixed';\n  disabled?: boolean;\n  expanded?: boolean;\n  readonly?: boolean;\n}\n\n// Captures full a11y tree from a CDP session\nexport async function captureAccessibilityTree(\n  cdpSend: (method: string, params?: object) => Promise<unknown>,\n  sessionId: string\n): Promise<AXNode[]>;\n\n// Serializes AXNode tree to human-readable text (for LLM consumption)\nexport function serializeTree(nodes: AXNode[], indent?: number): string;\n```\n\n### 2. `src/snapshot/element-resolver.ts`\nImplement Fix 4 — stable element references. The key insight: `backendNodeId` from CDP IS stable. The problem is the uid assignment layer clears on every new snapshot.\n\n```typescript\nexport class ElementResolver {\n  // Cumulative maps — persist across snapshots within same page, clear on navigation\n  private uidToBackendId = new Map<number, number>();\n  private backendIdToUid = new Map<number, number>();\n  private nextUid = 1;\n\n  // Assign stable uids to AX nodes. Reuses existing uids for known backendNodeIds.\n  assignRefs(nodes: AXNode[]): void;\n  \n  // Resolve a uid to a backendNodeId for interaction\n  resolve(uid: number): number | undefined;\n  \n  // Clear on page navigation\n  onNavigation(): void;\n  \n  // Get stats for debugging\n  get stats(): { totalRefs: number; nextUid: number };\n}\n```\n\n### 3. `src/snapshot/token-optimizer.ts`\nImplement Fix 7 — reduce snapshot size for LLM consumption.\n\n```typescript\nexport class TokenOptimizer {\n  // Filter out decorative/structural roles that add no value for LLMs\n  static filterRoles(nodes: AXNode[]): AXNode[];\n  \n  // Collapse empty container nodes (no name, no interactive children)\n  static collapseEmpty(nodes: AXNode[]): AXNode[];\n  \n  // Truncate long text content (cap at maxLen chars)\n  static truncateText(nodes: AXNode[], maxLen?: number): AXNode[];\n  \n  // Limit tree depth (only expand nodes up to N levels)\n  static limitDepth(nodes: AXNode[], maxDepth?: number): AXNode[];\n  \n  // Combined optimization pipeline\n  static optimize(nodes: AXNode[], options?: {\n    maxTokenBudget?: number;  // Target size in chars (default: 20000)\n    maxDepth?: number;         // Max tree depth (default: 6)\n    maxTextLen?: number;       // Max text per node (default: 100)\n  }): AXNode[];\n}\n```\n\nThe SKIP_ROLES set should include: 'generic', 'none', 'presentation', 'separator', 'LineBreak', 'InlineTextBox'. StaticText nodes should be merged into their parent's name when possible.\n\nVerify compilation: `cd \"E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server\" && npx tsc --noEmit`\n\nNote: Create inline types if `../types.js` doesn't exist yet (other agent creating it in parallel).\n\nWhen done:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id = 'extract-snapshot'\n```"
}
````

{

  "prompt": "Apply quick behavioral fixes directly to the existing server.js file.\n\nWorking directory: `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server`\nBranch: `dev/v5-restructure` (already checked out)\n\nThese are simple value/behavior changes that improve the server immediately without restructuring.\n\n## Fix 2: Default cleanupStrategy to \"detach\"\n\nIn server.js, find where the default cleanup strategy is set. It's likely a constant or in the session creation logic. Change the default from `\"close\"` to `\"detach\"` so tabs DON'T auto-close when agent sessions expire.\n\nSearch for: `cleanupStrategy`, `\"close\"`, `cleanup` in server.js.\nThe default is probably in a line like `const cleanupStrategy = args.cleanupStrategy || \"close\"` or similar.\nChange it to `args.cleanupStrategy || \"detach\"`.\n\nAlso look in the tool input schemas — the cleanupStrategy enum description should note that \"detach\" is now the default.\n\n## Fix 6: Increase default timeout to 60s\n\nIn server.js, find:\n```javascript\nconst CDP_TIMEOUT = parseInt(process.env.CDP_TIMEOUT) || 30000;\n```\nChange to:\n```javascript\nconst CDP_TIMEOUT = parseInt(process.env.CDP_TIMEOUT) || 60000;\n```\n\nAlso look for any navigation-specific timeouts that should be increased.\n\n## Fix 10: Filter Chrome extension console errors\n\nIn server.js, find where console errors are appended to tool responses. There's likely a function that collects console messages and adds a `### Console Errors` section.\n\nAdd filtering to skip errors originating from Chrome extensions:\n\n```javascript\n// Before adding console errors to response, filter out extension noise\nconst EXTENSION_PATTERNS = [\n  /chrome-extension:\\/\\//,\n  /moz-extension:\\/\\//,\n  /^Uncaught.*extension/i,\n  /^Failed to load resource.*chrome-extension/,\n];\n\nfunction isExtensionError(log) {\n  return EXTENSION_PATTERNS.some(pat => \n    pat.test(log.text || '') || pat.test(log.url || '') || pat.test(log.source || '')\n  );\n}\n```\n\nApply this filter wherever console errors are collected/displayed. The filtered errors should simply be dropped, not shown to agents.\n\n## Verification\nAfter making changes, verify:\n1. The server still starts: `node server.js` (briefly, then Ctrl+C — it's an STDIO server so just check it doesn't crash immediately)\n2. No syntax errors in the file\n\nWhen done:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id = 'apply-quick-fixes'\n```"
}

2. **`src/snapshot/element-resolver.ts`** — `ElementResolver` class with **cumulative** uid↔backendNodeId maps that persist across snapshots (Fix 4), reusing uids for known elements and clearing only on navigation
3. **`src/snapshot/token-optimizer.ts`** — `TokenOptimizer` with `filterRoles` (skips generic/none/presentation/separator/LineBreak/InlineTextBox), `mergeStaticText`, `collapseEmpty`, `truncateText`, `limitDepth`, and a combined `optimize()` pipeline with progressive depth reduction (Fix 7)
```

### ✅ `task`

**Extract core tool modules**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Extract core tool modules",
  "mode": "background",
  "name": "extract-tools-core",
  "prompt": "Extract core tool infrastructure from the CDP Browser MCP Server monolith.\n\nWorking directory: `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server`\nBranch: `dev/v5-restructure` (already checked out)\n\nIMPORTANT: Other agents have already created these files (DO NOT overwrite them):\n- `src/types.ts` — TypeScript interfaces\n- `src/config.ts` — Configuration module  \n- `src/utils/error-handler.ts` — Actionable errors\n- `src/snapshot/accessibility.ts`, `src/snapshot/element-resolver.ts`, `src/snapshot/token-optimizer.ts`\n\nRead the existing files first to understand the types, then read `server.js` to understand the tool registration and dispatch system.\n\n## Task: Create 2 core tool infrastructure files\n\n### 1. `src/tools/registry.ts`\nA tool registration and dispatch system. Read server.js to see how tools are currently registered (look for `ListToolsRequestSchema` and `CallToolRequestSchema` handlers, and the big `TOOLS` array or tool definitions).\n\n```typescript\nimport type { ToolDefinition, ToolResult, ServerContext } from '../types.js';\n\nexport class ToolRegistry {\n  private tools = new Map<string, ToolDefinition>();\n\n  // Register a tool\n  register(tool: ToolDefinition): void {\n    this.tools.set(tool.name, tool);\n  }\n\n  // List all tools (for MCP ListTools response)\n  list(): Array<{ name: string; description: string; inputSchema: object }> {\n    return Array.from(this.tools.values()).map(t => ({\n      name: t.name,\n      description: t.description,\n      inputSchema: t.inputSchema,\n    }));\n  }\n\n  // Dispatch a tool call\n  async dispatch(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>, ctx: ServerContext): Promise<ToolResult> {\n    const tool = this.tools.get(name);\n    if (!tool) {\n      return {\n        content: [{ type: 'text', text: `Unknown tool: \"${name}\". Available: ${Array.from(this.tools.keys()).join(', ')}` }],\n        isError: true,\n      };\n    }\n    try {\n      return await tool.handler(ctx, args);\n    } catch (error) {\n      // Use wrapError from error-handler\n      const { wrapError } = await import('../utils/error-handler.js');\n      return wrapError(error).toToolResult();\n    }\n  }\n\n  get size(): number { return this.tools.size; }\n}\n```\n\n### 2. `src/tools/base-tool.ts`\nHelper functions for tool definition to reduce boilerplate:\n\n```typescript\nimport type { ToolDefinition, ToolHandler } from '../types.js';\n\n// Helper to define a tool with typed schema\nexport function defineTool(opts: {\n  name: string;\n  description: string;\n  inputSchema: object;\n  handler: ToolHandler;\n}): ToolDefinition {\n  return {\n    name: opts.name,\n    description: opts.description,\n    inputSchema: opts.inputSchema,\n    handler: opts.handler,\n  };\n}\n\n// Standard input schema properties used across many tools\nexport const COMMON_PARAMS = {\n  tabId: {\n    type: 'string' as const,\n    description: 'Tab ID from tabs.list or tabs.new.',\n  },\n  sessionId: {\n    type: 'string' as const,\n    description: 'Agent session ID for tab ownership. Default: auto-generated per process.',\n  },\n  cleanupStrategy: {\n    type: 'string' as const,\n    enum: ['close', 'detach', 'none'],\n    description: \"Tab cleanup on session expiry. 'detach' (default) keeps tabs open, 'close' removes them, 'none' skips cleanup.\",\n  },\n  exclusive: {\n    type: 'boolean' as const,\n    description: 'Lock tab to this session (default: true). Set false for shared access.',\n  },\n  timeout: {\n    type: 'number' as const,\n    description: 'Timeout in milliseconds. Overrides default for this call.',\n  },\n};\n\n// Helper to create a success ToolResult\nexport function success(text: string): { content: Array<{ type: 'text'; text: string }>; isError?: false } {\n  return { content: [{ type: 'text', text }] };\n}\n\n// Helper to create an image ToolResult  \nexport function imageResult(base64: string, mimeType: string = 'image/png'): { content: Array<{ type: string; data: string; mimeType: string }> } {\n  return { content: [{ type: 'image', data: base64, mimeType }] };\n}\n\n// Helper to merge common params into an input schema\nexport function withCommonParams(schema: object, ...params: (keyof typeof COMMON_PARAMS)[]): object {\n  const properties = { ...(schema as any).properties };\n  for (const p of params) {\n    properties[p] = COMMON_PARAMS[p];\n  }\n  return { ...schema, properties };\n}\n```\n\nVerify compilation: `cd \"E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server\" && npx tsc --noEmit`\n\nWhen done:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id = 'extract-tools-core'\n```"
}
````

### ✅ `task`

**Create entry point index.ts**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Create entry point index.ts",
  "mode": "background",
  "name": "create-entry-point",
  "prompt": "Create the new entry point `src/index.ts` that wires together all extracted modules.\n\nWorking directory: `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server`\nBranch: `dev/v5-restructure` (already checked out)\n\n## Existing modules (read them first to understand imports/exports):\n- `src/types.ts` — All TypeScript interfaces\n- `src/config.ts` — `loadConfig()` returns `ServerConfig`\n- `src/connection/cdp-client.ts` — `CDPClient` class\n- `src/connection/browser-discovery.ts` — `discoverBrowserInstances()`, `findBestInstance()`\n- `src/connection/health-monitor.ts` — `HealthMonitor` class with `autoConnect()`\n- `src/tools/registry.ts` — `ToolRegistry` class\n- `src/tools/base-tool.ts` — `defineTool()`, `success()`, helpers\n- `src/utils/error-handler.ts` — `ActionableError`, `Errors`, `wrapError()`\n- `src/snapshot/accessibility.ts` — `captureAccessibilityTree()`, `serializeTree()`\n- `src/snapshot/element-resolver.ts` — `ElementResolver` class\n- `src/snapshot/token-optimizer.ts` — `TokenOptimizer` class\n\n## Task: Create `src/index.ts`\n\nThis is the NEW entry point that replaces `server.js`. For now it should:\n\n1. **Initialize the MCP server** using `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`\n2. **Load config** via `loadConfig()`\n3. **Create a `ServerContext`** object wiring all modules together\n4. **Set up auto-connect** — on first tool call, if not connected, auto-discover browser and connect\n5. **Register placeholder tools** — for now, register a few demo tools that show the system works:\n   - `ping` — returns \"pong\" + connection status + server version (useful for testing)\n   - `status` — returns server status: connected/disconnected, browser name/port, active sessions, config values\n6. **Handle MCP lifecycle** — ListTools, CallTool with auto-connect wrapper\n7. **Start STDIO transport**\n\nThe key pattern from the restructure plan:\n```typescript\nimport { Server } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/index.js\";\nimport { StdioServerTransport } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js\";\nimport { ListToolsRequestSchema, CallToolRequestSchema } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js\";\nimport { loadConfig } from './config.js';\nimport { CDPClient } from './connection/cdp-client.js';\nimport { HealthMonitor } from './connection/health-monitor.js';\nimport { ToolRegistry } from './tools/registry.js';\nimport { defineTool, success } from './tools/base-tool.js';\nimport { Errors } from './utils/error-handler.js';\nimport type { ServerContext } from './types.js';\n\nconst config = loadConfig();\nconst cdpClient = new CDPClient(config);\nconst healthMonitor = new HealthMonitor(cdpClient, config);\nconst registry = new ToolRegistry();\n\n// Build context\nconst ctx: ServerContext = {\n  config,\n  cdpClient,\n  healthMonitor,\n  registry,\n};\n\n// Auto-connect wrapper — called before every tool dispatch\nasync function ensureConnected(): Promise<void> {\n  if (cdpClient.isConnected) return;\n  await healthMonitor.autoConnect();\n}\n\n// Register tools\nregistry.register(defineTool({\n  name: 'ping',\n  description: 'Health check. Returns server version and connection status.',\n  inputSchema: { type: 'object', properties: {} },\n  handler: async () => success(`pong — CDP Browser MCP v5.0.0-alpha.1\\nConnected: ${cdpClient.isConnected}`),\n}));\n\nregistry.register(defineTool({\n  name: 'status',\n  description: 'Server status: connection, config, sessions.',\n  inputSchema: { type: 'object', properties: {} },\n  handler: async () => {\n    const lines = [\n      `## CDP Browser MCP Server v5.0.0-alpha.1`,\n      `Connected: ${cdpClient.isConnected}`,\n      `Default timeout: ${config.globalTimeout}ms`,\n      `Default cleanup: ${config.defaultCleanupStrategy}`,\n      `Session TTL: ${config.sessionTTL}ms`,\n      `Registered tools: ${registry.size}`,\n    ];\n    return success(lines.join('\\n'));\n  },\n}));\n\n// MCP Server setup\nconst server = new Server({ name: 'cdp-browser', version: '5.0.0-alpha.1' }, {\n  capabilities: { tools: {} },\n});\n\nserver.setRequestHandler(ListToolsRequestSchema, async () => ({\n  tools: registry.list(),\n}));\n\nserver.setRequestHandler(CallToolRequestSchema, async (request) => {\n  const { name, arguments: args } = request.params;\n  // Auto-connect on first tool call\n  try {\n    await ensureConnected();\n  } catch {\n    // If auto-connect fails, still dispatch — tool may not need connection (like ping/status)\n  }\n  return registry.dispatch(name, args ?? {}, ctx);\n});\n\n// Start\nconst transport = new StdioServerTransport();\nawait server.connect(transport);\n```\n\nAdapt the above to work with the actual interfaces from the existing modules. Read each module's exports to make sure imports are correct.\n\nAlso verify the `ServerContext` interface in `src/types.ts` matches what you're constructing — if it doesn't, update `types.ts` to match.\n\nVerify:\n1. `npx tsc` builds successfully (creates dist/ folder)\n2. `node dist/index.js` starts without crashing (it'll hang waiting for STDIO input, which is correct)\n\nWhen done:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id = 'create-entry-point'\n```"
}
````

Every file in `src/` must compile cleanly.\n\n## Step 2: Verify Legacy Server Still Works\nRun:\n```\nnode -e \"import('./server.js').catch(e => { console.error('FAIL:', e.message); process.exit(1) })\" \n```\nOr simply check syntax: `node --check server.js`\nThe old server.js must still work (it's the fallback).\n\n## Step 3: Verify New Entry Point\nRun:\n```\nnode -e \"import('./dist/index.js').then(() => setTimeout(() => process.exit(0), 2000)).catch(e => { console.error('FAIL:', e.message); process.exit(1) })\"\n```\nThe new dist/index.js should start without crashing.\n\n## Step 4: Quick Self-Review\nCheck for obvious issues:\n- Any files importing from paths that don't exist?\n- Any circular dependencies?\n- Any `any` types that could be tightened?\n- Do all the module barrel exports work?\n\n## Step 5: Verify directory structure looks right\n```\nGet-ChildItem -Recurse src/ -Filter *.ts | Select-Object FullName\n```\nExpected ~15 TypeScript files across src/connection/, src/tools/, src/snapshot/, src/utils/, and root.\n\n## Step 6: Add dist/ to .gitignore\nCheck if `.gitignore` in the MCP Server folder (or repo root) already ignores `dist/`. If not, add it.\n\n## Step 7: Commit in logical batches\nUse `git add` and `git commit` from the REPO ROOT (`E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation`).\n\nCommit 1 — Project setup:\n```\ngit add \"MCP Server/tsconfig.json\" \"MCP Server/package.json\" \"MCP Server/package-lock.json\"\ngit commit -m \"build: set up TypeScript build pipeline for v5.0.0\n\n- Add tsconfig.json with strict mode, ES2022 target\n- Update package.json: version 5.0.0-alpha.1, add build/test/lint scripts\n- Add dev dependencies: typescript, vitest, @types/ws, @types/node\n- Keep server.js as legacy fallback (start:legacy script)\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>\"\n```\n\nCommit 2 — Core modules:\n```\ngit add \"MCP Server/src/\"\ngit commit -m \"feat: extract modular TypeScript architecture from monolith\n\nNew modular structure (v5.0.0-alpha.1):\n- src/types.ts: 14 TypeScript interfaces (ServerConfig, ToolResult, AXNode, etc.)\n- src/config.ts: Typed config with tiered timeouts and detach default\n- src/connection/: CDPClient, browser discovery, health monitor with auto-reconnect\n- src/snapshot/: A11y tree capture, stable element refs, token optimizer\n- src/tools/: Tool registry and base-tool helpers\n- src/utils/: Actionable error handler with 17 error types\n- src/index.ts: New entry point with auto-connect and DI context\n\nKey improvements over <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>\"\n```\n\nCommit 3 — Quick fixes to legacy server.js:\n```\ngit add \"MCP Server/server.js\"\ngit commit -m \"fix: apply critical fixes to legacy <sub>⏱️ 63m 37s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

## ✅ Phase 1 Implementation Complete

**Branch:** `dev/v5-restructure` — 6 commits, clean working tree

### What was built:

| Module | Files | Key Changes |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| **Foundation** | `types.ts`, `config.ts` | 14 interfaces, tiered timeouts, detach default |
| **Connection** | 4 files in `connection/` | CDP client, browser auto-discovery, auto-reconnect |
| **Snapshot** | 3 files in `snapshot/` | A11y capture, stable element refs (Fix 4), token optimizer (Fix 7) |
| **Tools** | 2 files in `tools/` | Registry + base-tool helpers |
| **Utils** | `error-handler.ts` | 17 actionable error types (Fix 9) |
| **Entry** | `index.ts` | Auto-connect, DI context, MCP wiring |
| **Legacy fixes** | `server.js` patches | Detach default (Fix 2), 60s timeout (Fix 6), extension error filter (Fix 10) |
| **Research** | 6 docs in `.research/` | 206KB of competitor analysis + restructure plan |

### Fixes implemented:
- **Fix 1:** Auto-connect on first tool call
- **Fix 2:** Default cleanup → `detach` (tabs never auto-close)
- **Fix 4:** Stable element refs (cumulative uid mapping)
- **Fix 6:** Timeout increased to 60s
- **Fix 7:** Token optimizer for smaller snapshots
- **Fix 8:** Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff
- **Fix 9:** Actionable errors with fix suggestions
- **Fix 10:** Chrome extension errors filtered

### What's next (Phase 2):
Extract the actual 11 tool handlers from the 254KB `server.js` into individual TypeScript modules, wire them to the new registry, and make `src/index.ts` the primary entry point replacing `server.js` entirely.

<details>

````
707. ## 4. Architecture Migration — Phase 2 (Week 3–4)
708. 
709. ### Migration Strategy: Incremental Extraction
710. 
711. The key constraint is **zero downtime** — the server must work at every commit. We extract modules one at a time from `server.js`, with each extraction being a self-contained PR.
712. 
713. ### Step-by-Step Migration Order
714. 
715. ```
716. Week 3:
717.   Day 1-2: Project setup (TypeScript, build pipeline, package.json updates)
718.   Day 3:   Extract types.ts + config.ts
719.   Day 4:   Extract connection/ (cdp-client.ts, browser-discovery.ts)
720.   Day 5:   Extract session/ (session-manager.ts, tab-ownership.ts, state-store.ts)
721. 
722. Week 4:
723.   Day 1:   Extract snapshot/ (accessibility.ts, element-resolver.ts)
724.   Day 2:   Extract utils/ (wait.ts, retry.ts, temp-files.ts, error-handler.ts)
725.   Day 3-4: Extract tools/ (one tool module per half-day)
726.   Day 5:   Extract index.ts (entry point), remove server.js, verify everything works
727. ```
728. 
729. ### Project Setup
730. 
731. ```json
732. // package.json changes
733. {
734.   "name": "cdp-mcp-server",
735.   "version": "5.0.0",
736.   "type": "module",
737.   "main": "dist/index.js",
738.   "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
739.   "scripts": {
740.     "build": "tsc",
741.     "start": "node dist/index.js",
742.     "dev": "tsc --watch",
743.     "test": "vitest",
744.     "test:integration": "vitest run --config vitest.integration.config.ts",
745.     "lint": "eslint src/",
746.     "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
747.   },
748.   "devDependencies": {
749.     "typescript": "^5.5.0",
750.     "vitest": "^2.0.0",
751.     "@types/ws": "^8.0.0",
752.     "eslint": "^9.0.0",
753.     "@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin": "^8.0.0"
754.   },
755.   "dependencies": {
756.     "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.0.0",
757.     "ws": "^8.0.0"
758.   }
759. }
760. ```
761. 
762. ### Module Extraction Pattern
763. 
764. For each module extraction:
765. 
766. 1. **Create the new file** with the extracted code
767. 2. **Add TypeScript types** to all function signatures
768. 3. **Replace global state** with injected context parameters
769. 4. **Update imports** in the remaining `server.js`
770. 5. **Run the test suite** to verify no regressions
771. 6. **Commit with clear message**: `refactor: extract {module} from server.js`
772. 
773. ### Example: Extracting `config.ts`
774. 
775. ```typescript
776. // src/config.ts
777. export interface ServerConfig {
778.   cdpHost: string;
779.   cdpPort: number;
780.   actionTimeout: number;
781.   navigationTimeout: number;
782.   snapshotTimeout: number;
783.   sessionTTL: number;
784.   debuggerTimeout: number;
785.   cleanupStrategy: 'close' | 'detach' | 'none';
786.   tempDir: string;
787.   maxTempFiles: number;
788.   maxTempAgeMs: number;
789.   maxInlineLen: number;
790. }
791. 
792. export function loadConfig(): ServerConfig {
793.   return {
794.     cdpHost: process.env.CDP_HOST || '127.0.0.1',
795.     cdpPort: parseInt(process.env.CDP_PORT || '9222'),
796.     actionTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_ACTION_TIMEOUT || '10000'),
797.     navigationTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT || '60000'),
798.     snapshotTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_SNAPSHOT_TIMEOUT || '15000'),
799.     sessionTTL: parseInt(process.env.CDP_SESSION_TTL || '300000'),
800.     debuggerTimeout: parseInt(process.env.CDP_DEBUGGER_TIMEOUT || '30000'),
801.     cleanupStrategy: (process.env.CDP_CLEANUP_STRATEGY as any) || 'detach',
802.     tempDir: process.env.CDP_TEMP_DIR || join(REPO_ROOT, '.temp'),
803.     maxTempFiles: 50,
804.     maxTempAgeMs: 30 * 60 * 1000,
805.     maxInlineLen: 60_000,
806.   };
807. }
808. ```
809. 
810. ### Backward Compatibility
811. 
812. During migration, maintain a **compatibility shim**:
813. 
814. ```typescript
815. // server.js (during migration period)
816. // Re-exports from new modular code — maintains backward compatibility
817. import { createServer } from './dist/index.js';
818. createServer();
819. ```
820. 
821. After full migration, `server.js` is replaced by `dist/index.js`.
822. 
823. ### Testing Each Extraction
824. 
825. Every module extraction must pass:
826. 
827. 1. **Compile check**: `tsc --noEmit` passes
828. 2. **Existing behavior**: Manual smoke test — connect, list tabs, navigate, snapshot, click
829. 3. **Unit tests**: New tests for the extracted module (target: 80% coverage per module)
830. 
831. ---
832. 
833. ## 5. New Features — Phase 3 (Week 5–8)
834. 
835. ### Feature 1: Smart Form Filling Engine
836. 
837. **Priority**: CRITICAL — This is the #1 UX gap vs Playwright MCP.
838. 
839. A new `form` tool that handles ALL field types in a single call:
840. 
841. ```typescript
842. // New tool: form
843. {
844.   name: "form",
845.   description: "Smart form filling — handles text, combobox, checkbox, radio, date, file, and multi-select fields. Automatically detects field type from accessibility role.",
846.   inputSchema: {
847.     action: "fill",     // fill | read | clear | validate
848.     tabId: "string",
849.     fields: [{
850.       uid: "number",           // Element ref from snapshot
851.       value: "string",         // Value to fill
852.       type: "auto"             // auto | text | combobox | checkbox | radio | date | select | file
853.     }]
854.   }
855. }
856. ```
857. 
858. **Field type handling**:
859. 
860. | Type | Strategy |
861. |------|----------|
862. | `text`, `textarea` | `DOM.focus` → `Input.insertText` with React event simulation |
863. | `combobox` | Type → wait for dropdown → find best match → click option |
864. | `checkbox` | Toggle via click if current state ≠ desired state |
865. | `radio` | Click the radio button |
866. | `select` (native) | `DOM.setAttributeValue` + change event |
867. | `date` | Type in ISO format OR use date picker navigation |
868. | `file` | Trigger file chooser via CDP |
869. | `multi-select` | Click each option to toggle |
870. 
871. **Auto-detection**: Read the accessibility tree role (`textbox`, `combobox`, `checkbox`, `radio`, `listbox`, `spinbutton`, `slider`) and dispatch to the correct handler.
872. 
873. ---
874. 
875. ### Feature 2: Profile-Aware Tab Creation
876. 
877. **Problem**: Can't open tabs in a specific browser profile. Everything opens in whatever profile is focused.
878. 
879. ```typescript
880. // tabs.new with profile targeting
881. {
882.   action: "new",
883.   url: "https://mail.google.com",
884.   profile: "Work"  // or email "john@company.com" or directory "Profile 2"
885. }
886. ```
887. 
888. **Implementation**: Use `Target.createBrowserContext` with the target profile's cookies/storage, or use `browserContextId` from CDP's `Target.createTarget`.
889. 
890. ---
891. 
892. ### Feature 3: Lazy Initialization
893. 
894. **Problem**: Server initializes all domains and event listeners on connect, even if most tools won't be used.
895. 
896. **Solution**: Enable CDP domains only when the corresponding tool is first called:
897. 
898. ```typescript
899. class LazyDomainManager {
900.   private enabled = new Set<string>();
````

</details>

<details>

```
1130.     url = meta.result?.value?.url || "";
1131.     title = meta.result?.value?.title || "";
1132.   } catch {}
1133. 
1134.   return { snapshot, count: refCounter, url, title };
1135. }
1136. 
1137. // ─── Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1138. 
1139. function ok(t) {
1140.   const text = typeof t === "string" ? t : JSON.stringify(t, null, 2);
1141.   if (text.length > MAX_INLINE_LEN) {
1142.     const file = writeTempFile(`output-${Date.now()}.txt`, text);
1143.     return {
1144.       content: [{ type: "text", text: `Output too large (${text.length} chars). Saved to: ${file}\n\nFirst 2000 chars:\n${text.substring(0, 2000)}` }],
1145.     };
1146.   }
1147.   return { content: [{ type: "text", text }] };
1148. }
1149. 
1150. function fail(msg) {
1151.   return { content: [{ type: "text", text: `Error: ${msg}` }], isError: true };
1152. }
1153. 
1154. function sleep(ms) {
1155.   return new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, ms));
1156. }
1157. 
1158. function randomDelay(baseMs) {
1159.   // Returns random value in range [baseMs, baseMs * 3]
1160.   return baseMs + Math.random() * (baseMs * 2);
1161. }
1162. 
1163. /**
1164.  * Generate a natural-looking mouse path using a randomized cubic bezier curve.
1165.  * Includes slight overshoot, speed variation, and jitter for anti-detection.
1166.  * @param {{x,y}} from - start point
1167.  * @param {{x,y}} to - end point
1168.  * @param {number} [steps=20] - number of intermediate points
1169.  * @param {number} [jitter=2] - max random pixel offset per point
1170.  * @returns {{x:number, y:number}[]} array of {x, y} points along the path
1171.  */
1172. function generateBezierPath(from, to, steps = 20, jitter = 2) {
1173.   const dx = to.x - from.x;
1174.   const dy = to.y - from.y;
1175.   const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
1176.   // Control point spread scales with distance
1177.   const spread = Math.min(dist * 0.3, 100);
1178.   const cp1 = {
1179.     x: from.x + dx * 0.2 + (Math.random() - 0.5) * spread,
1180.     y: from.y + dy * 0.2 + (Math.random() - 0.5) * spread,
```
