E:\Projects\oh-my-universal\skills\ultrawork.md

<details>
<summary>88 lines</summary>

````
1. # Skill: ultrawork
2. 
3. > Full task lifecycle: plan -> implement -> verify -> review -> commit.
4. > The "do everything right" workflow.
5. > Inspired by: oh-my-codex ($ultrawork), oh-my-claudecode (ultrawork)
6. 
7. ## When to Trigger
8. 
9. - User says "ultrawork", "do this properly", "full workflow"
10. - For any significant task that warrants the full treatment
11. 
12. ## Workflow
13. 
14. ```
15. 1. PLAN        → Create structured plan (follow skills/plan.md workflow, or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it)
16. 2. IMPLEMENT   → Execute the plan, file by file
17. 3. VERIFY      → Run tests, linters, type-checkers (follow skills/verify.md workflow, or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it)
18. 4. REVIEW      → Self-review changes (follow skills/review.md workflow, or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it)
19. 5. DOCUMENT    → Update relevant docs (follow skills/doc-maintainer.md workflow, or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it)
20. 6. COMMIT      → Suggest committing in logical batches with good messages
21. ```
22. 
23. ### Step 1 — Plan
24. Follow the workflow in skills/plan.md (or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it). Create a structured plan, self-critique it,
25. and share with the user for approval (unless the user asked for autonomous execution).
26. 
27. ### Step 2 — Implement
28. Execute the plan in order. For each file:
29. - Make the change
30. - Verify it's consistent with the plan
31. - If a surprise comes up, pause and revise the plan
32. 
33. ### Step 3 — Verify
34. Run the project's test/lint/build commands:
35. - Test suite (project's test command)
36. - Linter (project's lint command)
37. - Build (project's build command)
38. - Type checking if available
39. - If nothing exists, do a manual sanity check
40. 
41. ### Step 4 — Review
42. Follow the workflow in skills/review.md (or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it) on your own changes.
43. Fix any issues found before proceeding.
44. 
45. ### Step 5 — Document
46. Follow the workflow in skills/doc-maintainer.md (or invoke the named skill if the platform supports it) to update any docs affected by the changes.
47. This includes README, API docs, changelogs, progress trackers.
48. 
49. ### Step 6 — Commit
50. Suggest committing. Only commit if user approves or repo workflow permits autonomous commits.
51. - Group related changes together
52. - Write descriptive commit messages (not "fix stuff")
53. - Use conventional commits format: feat/fix/refactor/docs/chore
54. 
55. ## Rules
56. 
57. - If ANY step fails, stop and report. Don't push through broken code.
58. - The user can skip steps explicitly ("skip the review")
59. - For trivial tasks, suggest using plan+implement only (skip verify/review/doc)
60. - Always report what was done at the end
61. - When user invokes ultrawork, do NOT separately invoke plan — ultrawork includes planning. Plan skill is for standalone planning only.
62. 
63. ## Output Format
64. 
65. ```markdown
66. ## Ultrawork Report: {task title}
67. 
68. **Steps completed:** {N}/6
69. **Result:** COMPLETE / PARTIAL / BLOCKED
70. 
71. | Step | Status | Notes |
72. |------|--------|-------|
73. | Plan | ✓ done | {summary} |
74. | Implement | ✓ done | {N} files changed |
75. | Verify | ✓ pass / ✗ fail | {test results} |
76. | Review | ✓ clean / ⚠ warnings | {findings count} |
77. | Document | ✓ done / — skipped | {what was updated} |
78. | Commit | ✓ committed / ⏳ ready | {message} |
79. 
80. ### Changes
81. - {list of files and what changed}
82. ```
83. 
84. ## Not Responsible For
85. 
86. - Individual skill execution details (see each skill's own file)
87. - Deciding WHAT to build (user provides the task)
88.
````

</details>

378. function handleEvent(sessionId, method, params) {
379.   // ── Console messages ──
380.   if (method === "Runtime.consoleAPICalled") {
381.     const logs = consoleLogs.get(sessionId) || [];
382.     logs.push({
383.       level: params.type,
384.       text: params.args?.map(a => a.value ?? a.description ?? a.type).join(" ") || "",
385.       ts: Date.now(),
386.       url: params.stackTrace?.callFrames?.[0]?.url || "",
387.     });
388.     if (logs.length > 500) logs.splice(0, logs.length - 500);
389.     consoleLogs.set(sessionId, logs);
390.   }
391. 
392.   if (method === "Runtime.exceptionThrown") {
393.     const logs = consoleLogs.get(sessionId) || [];
394.     logs.push({
395.       level: "error",
396.       text: params.exceptionDetails?.exception?.description ||
397.             params.exceptionDetails?.text || "Unknown exception",
398.       ts: Date.now(),
399.       url: params.exceptionDetails?.url || "",
400.     });
401.     consoleLogs.set(sessionId, logs);
402.   }
403. 
404.   // ── Network requests ──
405.   if (method === "Network.requestWillBeSent") {
406.     const reqs = networkReqs.get(sessionId) || new Map();
407.     reqs.set(params.requestId, {
408.       id: params.requestId,
409.       url: params.request.url,
410.       method: params.request.method,
411.       type: params.type || "Other",
412.       ts: Date.now(),
413.       status: null,
414.       mimeType: null,
415.       size: null,
416.     });
417.     if (reqs.size > 300) {
418.       const first = reqs.keys().next().value;
419.       reqs.delete(first);
420.     }

```
.\CHANGELOG.md:53:- **Multi-target upload detection** — `interact.upload` (without uid/selector) now monitors BOTH the current page AND any popup windows that open for file chooser dialogs or `<input type="file">` elements. Handles Google Drive Picker, Dropbox file chooser, and other popup-based upload workflows automatically
.\CHANGELOG.md:54:- **`findAndSetFileInput()` helper** — searches page DOM (including shadow DOMs) for file inputs and sets files directly via `DOM.setFileInputFiles`, bypassing the need for a native file dialog
.\CHANGELOG.md:104:- **File chooser TDZ crash** — rewrote Promise pattern to extract handler variable outside constructor, fixing `ReferenceError: Cannot access 'chooserPromise' before initialization` that made the file chooser upload path 100% non-functional
.\CHANGELOG.md:105:- **Wrong CDP API for file chooser** — replaced `DOM.setFileInputFiles` with `Page.handleFileChooser({ action: "accept", files })`. `Page.fileChooserOpened` returns `{ frameId, mode }` not `backendNodeId`
.\CHANGELOG.md:106:- **File chooser race condition** — handler is now registered before `Page.setInterceptFileChooserDialog` is enabled, preventing missed events
.\CHANGELOG.md:116:- **File chooser dialog interception** — `interact.upload` can now be called without `uid`/`selector` to intercept the next OS file chooser dialog. Click the upload button first, then call upload with just `files` to handle custom upload buttons that trigger file choosers programmatically
.\.research\ChromeDevToolsMCP.md:246:| `upload_file` | `uid`, `filePath`, `includeSnapshot?` | Upload file via input or file chooser |
.\.research\CompetitiveAnalysis.md:79:| **File upload** | ⚠️ Basic `<input type=file>` only | ✅ Full (file chooser modal pattern) | ✅ Upload via uid | ❌ | ❌ |
.\.research\CompetitiveAnalysis.md:320:modalState = { type: 'fileChooser', clearedBy: 'interact.upload' };
.\README.md:291:| `upload` | Upload files to file input, intercept file chooser, or auto-detect popup file inputs (Google Picker, etc.) | `tabId`, `files[]` | `uid`, `selector`, `timeout` |
.\.research\PlaywrightMCP.md:69:    │   ├── files.ts                ← File upload (fileChooser modal)
.\.research\PlaywrightMCP.md:153:  clearsModalState: undefined  // or 'dialog' or 'fileChooser'

### ✅ `task`

**Port iframe + file chooser + events**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Port iframe + file chooser + events",
  "mode": "background",
  "name": "port-core-features",
  "prompt": "Port the 3 MOST CRITICAL missing features from server.js into the new TypeScript modules. These are the P0/P1 issues from the critic.\n\nWorking directory: `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server`\nBranch: `dev`\n\nRead `server.js` for the existing implementations, then port to the TypeScript modules.\n\n## Feature 1: Cross-Origin Iframe Support (P0)\n\n### What server.js does (lines 608-727, 911-1134):\n- On tab attach, calls `Target.setAutoAttach({ autoAttach: true, flatten: true, waitForDebuggerOnStart: false })`\n- Handles `Target.attachedToTarget` event — tracks child sessions (OOP iframes)\n- Handles `Target.detachedFromTarget` — cleans up child sessions\n- In `buildSnapshot`, captures AX trees from ALL frames (main + child sessions)\n- Serializes child frame content with `[frame N]` prefixes\n- Element refs work across frames by tracking which session owns which ref\n\n### Port to:\n\n**`src/connection/tab-session-service.ts`** — Add:\n```typescript\n// Track child sessions (OOP iframes) per tab\nprivate childSessions = new Map<string, Map<string, string>>(); // tabId → Map<childTargetId, childSessionId>\n\nasync setupAutoAttach(cdpClient: CDPClient, tabId: string): Promise<void> {\n  const sessionId = await this.getSession(cdpClient, tabId);\n  await cdpClient.send('Target.setAutoAttach', {\n    autoAttach: true, flatten: true, waitForDebuggerOnStart: false\n  }, sessionId);\n}\n\naddChildSession(parentTabId: string, childTargetId: string, childSessionId: string): void;\nremoveChildSession(parentTabId: string, childTargetId: string): void;\ngetChildSessions(tabId: string): Map<string, string>;\n```\n\n**`src/snapshot/accessibility.ts`** — Update `captureAccessibilityTree`:\n```typescript\n// After capturing main frame tree, also capture from child sessions\nexport async function captureFullTree(\n  cdpSend: Function, mainSessionId: string, childSessions: Map<string, string>\n): Promise<{ mainNodes: AXNode[], frameNodes: Map<string, AXNode[]> }>;\n```\n\n**`src/index.ts`** — Wire `Target.attachedToTarget` and `Target.detachedFromTarget` events:\n```typescript\ncdpClient.on('Target.attachedToTarget', (params, sessionId) => {\n  // Track child session\n  const tabId = findTabBySession(ctx, sessionId);\n  if (tabId && params.targetInfo?.type === 'iframe') {\n    ctx.tabSessions.addChildSession(tabId, params.targetInfo.targetId, params.sessionId);\n  }\n});\n```\n\n## Feature 2: File Chooser + Popup Upload (P1)\n\n### What server.js does (lines 632-640, 3469-3656):\n- Intercepts `Page.fileChooserOpened` events\n- Supports 3 upload modes:\n  1. Direct: uid/selector → `DOM.setFileInputFiles`\n  2. File chooser: `Page.handleFileChooser({ action: \"accept\", files })`\n  3. Popup: monitors new windows/tabs for file inputs (Google Picker, Dropbox)\n- `findAndSetFileInput()` traverses shadow DOMs to find hidden file inputs\n- Enables `Page.setInterceptFileChooserDialog({ enabled: true })`\n\n### Port to `src/tools/interact.ts`:\nUpdate the upload handler to support all 3 modes. The current code only does mode 1 (direct input). Add:\n- File chooser interception via `Page.setInterceptFileChooserDialog`\n- `Page.handleFileChooser` for chooser-triggered uploads\n- Popup window detection for third-party file pickers\n- Shadow DOM traversal for hidden inputs\n\nAlso wire `Page.fileChooserOpened` → `ctx.modalStates.setModal()` in `src/index.ts`.\n\n## Feature 3: CDP Event Pipeline (P1)\n\n### What server.js does (lines 378-640):\nThe `handleEvent()` function routes ALL CDP events:\n- `Runtime.consoleAPICalled` → consoleLogs Map\n- `Runtime.exceptionThrown` → consoleLogs Map\n- `Network.requestWillBeSent/responseReceived/loadingFinished/loadingFailed` → networkReqs Map\n- `Page.downloadWillBegin/downloadProgress` → downloads Map\n- `Page.javascriptDialogOpening/Closed` → pendingDialogs Map\n- `Page.fileChooserOpened` → pendingFileChoosers Map\n- `Debugger.paused/resumed` → pausedTabs Map\n- `Target.attachedToTarget/detachedFromTarget` → child session tracking\n- `Fetch.requestPaused` → pendingFetchRequests Map\n\n### Port to `src/index.ts`:\nCreate a `wireEventPipeline(ctx)` function that subscribes to ALL these CDP events and populates the appropriate state stores in ServerContext.\n\nYou'll need to add state stores to `src/types.ts` ServerContext:\n```typescript\nconsoleLogs: Map<string, Array<{level: string, text: string, ts: number, url: string}>>;\nnetworkReqs: Map<string, Map<string, any>>;\ndownloads: Map<string, any>;\npendingDialogs: Map<string, any>;\npendingFileChoosers: Map<string, any[]>;\npausedTabs: Map<string, any>;\npendingFetchRequests: Map<string, any>;\n```\n\nInitialize all of them in `src/index.ts`.\n\n## After all 3 features:\n- `npx tsc --noEmit` must pass\n- Commit:\n```\ngit commit -m \"feat: port iframe support, file chooser, CDP event pipeline from server.js\n\n- Cross-origin iframe: Target.setAutoAttach, child session tracking, multi-frame snapshots\n- File chooser: Page.fileChooserOpened interception, popup upload, shadow DOM traversal\n- Event pipeline: console, network, download, dialog, debugger event routing\n- State stores added to ServerContext for observe/debug/intercept tools\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>\"\n```\n\nWhen done:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id IN ('fix-iframe', 'fix-filechooser', 'port-event-handling')\n```"
}
````

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Fix bugs + snapshot cache + platform",
  "mode": "background",
  "name": "fix-bugs-and-features",
  "prompt": "Fix the remaining bugs and implement missing features.\n\nWorking directory: `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server`\nBranch: `dev`\n\n## Fix 1: Modal event listener leak on reconnect\n\nIn `src/index.ts`, `wireModalEvents()` is called on every `connected` event, adding duplicate listeners.\n\n**Fix:** Track whether listeners are already wired:\n```typescript\nlet modalEventsWired = false;\n\nfunction wireModalEvents(ctx: ServerContext): void {\n  if (modalEventsWired) return; // Already wired — don't duplicate\n  modalEventsWired = true;\n  // ... existing listener registrations\n}\n\n// On disconnect, reset the flag so we re-wire on reconnect\ncdpClient.on('disconnected', () => {\n  modalEventsWired = false;\n  // ... existing disconnect handling\n});\n```\n\n## Fix 2: Guard JSON.parse in cdp-client.ts\n\nIn `src/connection/cdp-client.ts`, find the WebSocket `message` handler where `JSON.parse` is called.\n\n**Fix:** Wrap in try-catch:\n```typescript\nws.on('message', (data: WebSocket.Data) => {\n  let parsed: any;\n  try {\n    parsed = JSON.parse(data.toString());\n  } catch (err) {\n    this.emit('error', new Error(`Malformed CDP frame: ${data.toString().substring(0, 200)}`));\n    return; // Don't crash — skip malformed frame\n  }\n  // ... rest of message handling with `parsed`\n});\n```\n\n## Fix 3: Wire snapshot cache into page.snapshot\n\nIn `src/tools/page.ts`, find the snapshot handler. After capturing the snapshot:\n\n```typescript\n// After building the snapshot text:\n// Store in cache\nif (ctx.snapshotCache && tabId) {\n  ctx.snapshotCache.set(tabId, snapshotText, nodes, currentUrl);\n}\n\n// On navigation (in goto handler), invalidate:\nif (ctx.snapshotCache && tabId) {\n  ctx.snapshotCache.invalidate(tabId);\n}\n```\n\nAlso add a `diff` parameter to the snapshot action schema:\n```typescript\ndiff: { type: 'boolean', description: 'If true, return only changes since last snapshot on this tab.' }\n```\n\nWhen `diff: true`, check cache and return diff instead of full snapshot:\n```typescript\nif (args.diff && ctx.snapshotCache) {\n  const diffResult = ctx.snapshotCache.diff(tabId, snapshotText);\n  if (diffResult.changed) {\n    return ok(`### Snapshot Changes (${diffResult.added} added, ${diffResult.removed} removed)\\n${diffResult.lines.join('\\n')}`);\n  } else {\n    return ok('No visible changes since last snapshot.');\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Fix 4: Cross-platform browser discovery\n\nIn `src/connection/browser-discovery.ts`, find the browser candidate paths (currently Windows-only LOCALAPPDATA).\n\n**Fix:** Add platform detection:\n```typescript\nimport { platform, homedir } from 'os';\nimport { join } from 'path';\n\nfunction getBrowserCandidates(): Array<{name: string, path: string}> {\n  const os = platform();\n  const home = homedir();\n  \n  if (os === 'win32') {\n    const local = process.env.LOCALAPPDATA || join(home, 'AppData', 'Local');\n    return [\n      { name: 'Chrome', path: join(local, 'Google', 'Chrome', 'User Data') },\n      { name: 'Chrome Beta', path: join(local, 'Google', 'Chrome Beta', 'User Data') },\n      { name: 'Chrome Canary', path: join(local, 'Google', 'Chrome SxS', 'User Data') },\n      { name: 'Chromium', path: join(local, 'Chromium', 'User Data') },\n      { name: 'Edge', path: join(local, 'Microsoft', 'Edge', 'User Data') },\n      { name: 'Brave', path: join(local, 'BraveSoftware', 'Brave-Browser', 'User Data') },\n    ];\n  } else if (os === 'darwin') {\n    const appSupport = join(home, 'Library', 'Application Support');\n    return [\n      { name: 'Chrome', path: join(appSupport, 'Google', 'Chrome') },\n      { name: 'Chrome Canary', path: join(appSupport, 'Google', 'Chrome Canary') },\n      { name: 'Chromium', path: join(appSupport, 'Chromium') },\n      { name: 'Edge', path: join(appSupport, 'Microsoft Edge') },\n      { name: 'Brave', path: join(appSupport, 'BraveSoftware', 'Brave-Browser') },\n    ];\n  } else {\n    // Linux\n    return [\n      { name: 'Chrome', path: join(home, '.config', 'google-chrome') },\n      { name: 'Chromium', path: join(home, '.config', 'chromium') },\n      { name: 'Edge', path: join(home, '.config', 'microsoft-edge') },\n      { name: 'Brave', path: join(home, '.config', 'BraveSoftware', 'Brave-Browser') },\n    ];\n  }\n}\n```\n\nReplace the existing hardcoded candidates array with this function call.\n\n## Fix 5: Extension scaffold\n\nCreate `extension/` directory at repo root (`E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\extension/`) with:\n\n**`extension/manifest.json`**:\n```json\n{\n  \"manifest_version\": 3,\n  \"name\": \"CDP Browser MCP Bridge\",\n  \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n  \"description\": \"Connects CDP Browser MCP Server to your browser without --remote-debugging-port\",\n  \"permissions\": [\"debugger\", \"tabs\", \"activeTab\"],\n  \"host_permissions\": [\"<all_urls>\"],\n  \"background\": {\n    \"service_worker\": \"background.js\",\n    \"type\": \"module\"\n  },\n  \"action\": {\n    \"default_popup\": \"popup.html\",\n    \"default_icon\": {\n      \"16\": \"icons/icon16.png\",\n      \"48\": \"icons/icon48.png\",\n      \"128\": \"icons/icon128.png\"\n    }\n  },\n  \"icons\": {\n    \"16\": \"icons/icon16.png\",\n    \"48\": \"icons/icon48.png\",\n    \"128\": \"icons/icon128.png\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**`extension/background.js`**:\n```javascript\n// CDP Browser MCP Bridge — Chrome Extension Service Worker\n// This extension provides zero-config CDP access without --remote-debugging-port\n\nconst DEFAULT_PORT = 9333;\nlet wsServer = null;\nlet connections = new Map(); // tabId → chrome.debugger session\n\nconsole.log('[CDP MCP Bridge] Service worker started');\n\n// TODO: Phase B — implement WebSocket server for MCP connection\n// TODO: Phase C — implement CDP command forwarding via chrome.debugger\n// TODO: Phase D — auto-detection from MCP server side\n\n// For now, just a skeleton that logs tab activity\nchrome.tabs.onUpdated.addListener((tabId, changeInfo, tab) => {\n  if (changeInfo.status === 'complete') {\n    console.log(`[CDP MCP Bridge] Tab ${tabId} loaded: ${tab.url}`);\n  }\n});\n\nchrome.tabs.onRemoved.addListener((tabId) => {\n  connections.delete(tabId);\n  console.log(`[CDP MCP Bridge] Tab ${tabId} closed`);\n});\n```\n\n**`extension/popup.html`**:\n```html\n<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html>\n<head>\n  <meta charset=\"utf-8\">\n  <style>\n    body { width: 300px; padding: 16px; font-family: system-ui; }\n    h2 { margin: 0 0 8px; font-size: 16px; }\n    .status { padding: 8px; border-radius: 4px; margin: 8px 0; }\n    .connected { background: #e8f5e9; color: #2e7d32; }\n    .disconnected { background: #fce4ec; color: #c62828; }\n    .info { font-size: 12px; color: #666; margin-top: 12px; }\n  </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n  <h2>🌐 CDP Browser MCP</h2>\n  <div class=\"status disconnected\" id=\"status\">\n    ⏳ Extension loaded — MCP bridge not yet implemented\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"info\">\n    <p>This extension will let AI agents control your browser without needing chrome://flags or --remote-debugging-port.</p>\n    <p><strong>Status:</strong> Scaffold only (v0.1.0)</p>\n  </div>\n  <script src=\"popup.js\"></script>\n</body>\n</html>\n```\n\n**`extension/popup.js`**:\n```javascript\ndocument.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {\n  const status = document.getElementById('status');\n  // TODO: Query background.js for connection state\n  status.textContent = '⏳ Extension loaded — MCP bridge coming soon';\n});\n```\n\n**`extension/README.md`**:\n```markdown\n# CDP Browser MCP Bridge — Chrome Extension\n\nZero-config browser access for CDP Browser MCP Server.\n\n## Status: Scaffold (v0.1.0)\nThis is the extension skeleton. The actual CDP bridge is planned for v5.1.0.\n\n## How It Will Work\n1. Install this extension in Chrome/Edge/Brave\n2. The MCP Server auto-detects the extension\n3. No chrome://flags or --remote-debugging-port needed\n\n## Development\nSee `.research/ExtensionModePlan.md` for the full design.\n\n## Phases\n- [x] Phase A: Extension skeleton (manifest, service worker, popup)\n- [ ] Phase B: WebSocket bridge (CDP command forwarding)\n- [ ] Phase C: MCP Server integration (extension-client.ts)\n- [ ] Phase D: Auto-detection fallback chain\n- [ ] Phase E: Polish (settings, status UI)\n```\n\n## After all fixes:\n- `npx tsc --noEmit` must pass\n- Commit:\n```\ngit add -A\ngit commit -m \"fix: listener leak, JSON.parse guard, snapshot cache, cross-platform discovery, extension scaffold\n\n- Fix modal event listener leak on reconnect (register once)\n- Guard JSON.parse in CDP client (no crash on malformed frames)\n- Wire snapshot cache into page.snapshot (store, invalidate, diff support)\n- Add macOS/Linux browser discovery paths (cross-platform)\n- Scaffold Chrome extension (manifest v3, service worker, popup)\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>\"\n```\n\nWhen done:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id IN ('fix-listener-leak', 'fix-json-parse', 'fix-snapshot-cache', 'fix-cross-platform', 'extension-scaffold')\n```"
}
````

Agent is idle (waiting for messages). agent_id: fix-bugs-and-features, agent_type: general-purpose, status: idle, description: Fix bugs + snapshot cache + platform, elapsed: 469s, total_turns: 1

[Turn 0]
All 5 fixes committed. Here's a summary:

1. **Modal listener leak** — Added `modalEventsWired` flag; reset on disconnect, checked before wiring.
2. **JSON.parse guard** — Wrapped CDP message parsing in try-catch; emits error + skips malformed frames.
3. **Snapshot cache** — Wired `set()` after snapshot capture, `invalidate()` on goto navigation, added `diff` parameter for incremental snapshots.
4. **Cross-platform discovery** — Extracted `getBrowserCandidates()` with `platform()`/`homedir()` for Win/macOS/Linux paths.
5. **Extension scaffold** — Created `extension/` with manifest.json, background.js, popup.html, popup.js, and README.md.

Also fixed a pre-existing issue: initialized 7 missing `ServerContext` event pipeline stores so `tsc --noEmit` passes cleanly.
```

</details>

Agent is idle (waiting for messages). agent_id: port-core-features, agent_type: general-purpose, status: idle, description: Port iframe + file chooser + events, elapsed: 1009s, total_turns: 1

[Turn 0]
All 3 features ported successfully:

1. **Cross-Origin Iframe (P0)** — `TabSessionService` now tracks child sessions via `Target.setAutoAttach` (auto-enabled on session creation), handles `Target.attachedToTarget/detachedFromTarget` events, and `captureFullTree()` in accessibility.ts captures AX trees from all OOP iframes with `[frame N]` prefixes and uid→session routing.

2. **File Chooser + Popup Upload (P1)** — `handleUpload` now supports all 3 modes: direct input, `Page.handleFileChooser` interception, and popup window monitoring with polling + shadow DOM traversal. Wired `Page.fileChooserOpened` → modal state.

3. **CDP Event Pipeline (P1)** — `wireEventPipeline()` routes all CDP events (console, network, downloads, dialogs, fetch, debugger, Target attach/detach) to the ServerContext state stores.

✅ `tsc --noEmit` passes, 142/142 tests pass, committed on `dev`.
```

.\CHANGELOG.md:65:- **`tabs.new` `profile` parameter** — create new tabs in a specific Chrome profile by name, email, or directory (e.g. `profile: "Work"`, `profile: "mansha@gmail.com"`, `profile: "Profile 7"`). Resolves profile to `browserContextId` via cross-referencing `Local State` metadata with live tab contexts, then passes it to `Target.createTarget`
.\CHANGELOG.md:66:- **`resolveProfileContext()` helper** — new function maps profile name/email/directory to `browserContextId` by reading `Local State` profile cache and matching against live tab `browserContextId` values
.\CHANGELOG.md:69:- **`browser.active` profile names** — tabs-by-profile section now shows human-readable profile names (with email) instead of truncated browserContextId hashes, by cross-referencing `Local State` profile cache
.\CHANGELOG.md:236:- **`browser.active` action** — shows connected instance name, port, User Data dir, health, profiles from Local State, and tab count per `browserContextId` (profile grouping)
.\CHANGELOG.md:241:- **`tabs.info` shows `browserContextId`** — lets agents see which profile a tab belongs to
.\README.md:517:- **`browser.active`** — show current instance info, profiles, health, tabs per profile context
.\README.md:518:- **`tabs.new` with `profile`** — create tabs in a specific profile by name, email, or directory (e.g. `profile: "Work"`)
.\README.md:519:- **`tabs.info`** shows `browserContextId` for profile identification
.\README.md:696:A: Yes. The `browser` tool detects all running Chrome instances and their profiles. Use `browser.profiles` to see available instances and `browser.connect` to switch between them. Use `tabs.new` with the `profile` parameter to create tabs in a specific profile (e.g. `profile: "Work"` or `profile: "mansha@gmail.com"`). All Chrome profiles within one instance share a single debug port — each profile's tabs are distinguishable via `browserContextId` in `tabs.info`.

```
843. async function resolveProfileContext(profileQuery) {
844.   if (!profileQuery) return null;
845.   const q = profileQuery.toLowerCase();
846. 
847.   // Get live tabs to see which browserContextIds exist
848.   const tabs = await getTabs();
849.   const contextIds = new Set(tabs.map(t => t.browserContextId).filter(Boolean));
850.   if (contextIds.size === 0) return null;
851. 
852.   // Read Local State for profile metadata
853.   const udPath = activeConnectionInfo?.userDataDir || lastResolvedUserDataDir;
854.   if (!udPath) return null;
855.   let profileMap = []; // [{ directory, name, email, browserContextId? }]
856.   try {
857.     const localState = JSON.parse(readFileSync(join(udPath, "Local State"), "utf8"));
858.     const cache = localState?.profile?.info_cache || {};
859.     profileMap = Object.entries(cache).map(([dir, info]) => ({
860.       directory: dir,
861.       name: info.name || dir,
862.       gaiaName: info.gaia_name || "",
863.       email: info.user_name || "",
864.     }));
865.   } catch { return null; }
866. 
867.   // Chrome doesn't directly map profile directories to browserContextIds,
868.   // but each profile gets a unique context. If we have N profiles and N contexts,
869.   // we can match by ordering (Chrome assigns contexts in profile creation order).
870.   // More reliably: if a profile has ANY open tab, we can match it.
871.   // Strategy: group tabs by browserContextId, then match profiles by finding
872.   // a context whose tabs' URLs/titles align with the profile.
873.   // For exact matching: accept browserContextId directly as profileQuery.
874.   
875.   // Direct browserContextId match
876.   if (contextIds.has(profileQuery)) {
877.     const profile = profileMap.find(p => p.directory.toLowerCase() === q || p.name.toLowerCase() === q || p.email.toLowerCase() === q);
878.     return { browserContextId: profileQuery, profileName: profile?.name || profileQuery };
879.   }
880. 
881.   // Match by profile name, email, or directory
882.   const matchedProfile = profileMap.find(p =>
883.     p.name.toLowerCase() === q ||
884.     p.email.toLowerCase() === q ||
885.     p.directory.toLowerCase() === q ||
886.     p.gaiaName.toLowerCase() === q
887.   );
888.   if (!matchedProfile) return null;
889. 
890.   // If there's only one context, it must be this profile
891.   if (contextIds.size === 1) {
892.     const [ctxId] = contextIds;
893.     return { browserContextId: ctxId, profileName: matchedProfile.name };
894.   }
895. 
896.   // Multiple contexts — try to find which one belongs to this profile.
897.   // Group tabs by context, match profile index to context index as a heuristic.
898.   // Chrome typically assigns browserContextIds in profile creation order.
899.   const profileIndex = profileMap.indexOf(matchedProfile);
900.   const sortedContexts = [...contextIds].sort(); // consistent ordering
901.   if (profileIndex >= 0 && profileIndex < sortedContexts.length) {
902.     return { browserContextId: sortedContexts[profileIndex], profileName: matchedProfile.name };
903.   }
904. 
905.   // Fallback: return first available context with a warning
906.   return null;
907. }
908. 
909. // ─── Accessibility Tree / Snapshot ──────────────────────────────────
910.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 1482m 18s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Now I see the core issue clearly. Here's what's happening:

**The problem:** When you switch Chrome profiles (by clicking the profile avatar in Chrome), Chrome brings a DIFFERENT window to the foreground. CDP's `Target.createTarget` creates tabs **in whichever browser context is the "default"** — which is typically the LAST used profile. There's NO `browserContextId` being passed automatically, so new tabs go to whatever profile Chrome considers active.

**The fix needed:** Sessions should REMEMBER which profile/browserContextId they started in, and automatically create new tabs in that same context — even if the user manually switches profiles in Chrome.

---

<sub>⏱️ 1482m 18s</sub>

### ✅ `task`

**Fix profile-sticky sessions**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Fix profile-sticky sessions",
  "mode": "background",
  "name": "fix-profile-sessions",
  "prompt": "Fix the core profile isolation issue: when a user manually switches Chrome profiles, agent sessions should STICK to their original profile — not follow the user's focus.\n\nWorking directory: `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server`\nBranch: `dev`\n\n## The Problem\n1. Agent starts working → creates tabs in Profile A (e.g., \"Work\")\n2. User switches to Profile B (e.g., \"Personal\") in Chrome\n3. Agent creates a new tab → it opens in Profile B instead of Profile A\n4. Agent is now working in the WRONG profile with wrong cookies/auth\n\n## Root Cause\n`Target.createTarget` without `browserContextId` creates tabs in Chrome's \"default\" context, which changes when the user switches profiles. The agent session has no memory of which profile it started in.\n\n## The Fix: Profile-Sticky Sessions\n\n### 1. Update `src/session/session-manager.ts` — Add profile tracking\n\nAdd a `browserContextId` field to `AgentSession`:\n```typescript\nexport interface AgentSession {\n  id: string;\n  createdAt: number;\n  lastActivity: number;\n  cleanupStrategy: 'close' | 'detach' | 'none';\n  tabIds: Set<string>;\n  browserContextId?: string; // Profile this session is pinned to\n}\n```\n\n### 2. Update `src/tools/dispatch.ts` — Auto-detect and pin profile\n\nIn `preprocessToolCall`, after claiming a tab, detect the profile from the tab's `browserContextId` and pin it to the session:\n\n```typescript\n// After tab claiming (existing code)...\n\n// Auto-detect profile: if session doesn't have a profile yet, capture it from the first tab\nif (tabId && !session.browserContextId) {\n  try {\n    const targets = await ctx.cdpClient.send('Target.getTargets') as any;\n    const target = targets?.targetInfos?.find((t: any) => t.targetId === tabId);\n    if (target?.browserContextId) {\n      session.browserContextId = target.browserContextId;\n    }\n  } catch { /* non-critical */ }\n}\n```\n\n### 3. Update `src/tools/tabs.ts` — Auto-use session's profile for new tabs\n\nIn the `handleNew` function, if the session has a pinned `browserContextId` and no explicit `profile` param was given, automatically use the session's profile:\n\n```typescript\n// In handleNew, before Target.createTarget:\nconst createParams: Record<string, unknown> = { url, background };\n\nif (args.profile) {\n  // Explicit profile requested — use it (existing logic)\n  // ... existing profile resolution code ...\n} else if (session?.browserContextId) {\n  // No explicit profile — use session's pinned profile\n  createParams.browserContextId = session.browserContextId;\n}\n\nconst { targetId } = await ctx.cdpClient.send('Target.createTarget', createParams) as any;\n```\n\n### 4. Also update the server.js legacy version\n\nIn `server.js`, find `handleTabsNew` (around line 2306). Apply the same fix:\n\n```javascript\nasync function handleTabsNew(args) {\n  const url = args.url || \"about:blank\";\n  const background = args.activate === false || args.activate === undefined;\n  const createParams = { url, background };\n  \n  // Profile-aware tab creation\n  if (args.profile) {\n    const resolved = await resolveProfileContext(args.profile);\n    if (!resolved) return fail(`Profile \"${args.profile}\" not found...`);\n    createParams.browserContextId = resolved.browserContextId;\n  } else if (args._agentSession?.browserContextId) {\n    // AUTO: use session's pinned profile (prevents profile drift when user switches)\n    createParams.browserContextId = args._agentSession.browserContextId;\n  }\n  \n  const { targetId } = await cdp(\"Target.createTarget\", createParams);\n  \n  // Pin profile to session if not already pinned\n  if (args._agentSession && !args._agentSession.browserContextId) {\n    try {\n      const tabs = await getTabs();\n      const newTab = tabs.find(t => t.targetId === targetId);\n      if (newTab?.browserContextId) {\n        args._agentSession.browserContextId = newTab.browserContextId;\n      }\n    } catch { /* ok */ }\n  }\n  \n  // ... rest of existing code\n}\n```\n\nAlso update the session creation in server.js to track `browserContextId`:\nFind where `agentSessions.set()` creates session objects and add `browserContextId: null`.\n\n### 5. Update `src/tools/tabs.ts` — Show profile in tab listing\n\nWhen listing tabs, show which profile each tab belongs to:\n```typescript\n// In list handler, for each tab:\nconst profileCtx = tab.browserContextId \n  ? ` [profile:${tab.browserContextId.substring(0, 8)}]` \n  : '';\n```\n\n### 6. Add `profile` action to `tabs` tool for session profile management\n\nAdd a new action `set_profile` that lets agents explicitly pin their session to a profile:\n```typescript\n// New action: set_profile\n// tabs({ action: \"set_profile\", profile: \"Work\" })\n// Resolves the profile and pins the session to it\n```\n\nThis is useful when an agent wants to explicitly switch profiles.\n\n### 7. Update tool descriptions\n\nUpdate tabs tool description to include:\n```\n\"Sessions are automatically pinned to the Chrome profile where they first create or claim a tab. New tabs always open in the session's profile, even if you manually switch profiles in Chrome. Use tabs.new with profile:'Name' to override, or tabs.set_profile to change the session's default profile.\"\n```\n\n### 8. Add a unit test\n\nCreate or update `src/__tests__/unit/session-manager.test.ts`:\n```typescript\nit('should track browserContextId on session', () => {\n  const session = manager.getOrCreate('test-1');\n  expect(session.browserContextId).toBeUndefined();\n  session.browserContextId = 'ABC123';\n  const same = manager.getOrCreate('test-1');\n  expect(same.browserContextId).toBe('ABC123');\n});\n```\n\n## Verify\n- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes\n- `npx vitest run` passes  \n- Legacy `node --check server.js` passes\n\n## Commit\n```\ngit add -A\ngit commit -m \"feat: profile-sticky sessions — tabs stay in the right Chrome profile\n\nWhen an agent creates or claims a tab, the session automatically pins to that\ntab's Chrome profile (browserContextId). All subsequent tabs.new calls use the\npinned profile, preventing profile drift when the user manually switches\nprofiles in Chrome.\n\n- AgentSession tracks browserContextId\n- dispatch.ts auto-detects profile from first tab interaction\n- tabs.new auto-uses session's pinned profile (no explicit profile param needed)\n- Legacy server.js updated with same fix\n- Tool description explains profile pinning behavior\n- New set_profile action for explicit profile switching\n\nFixes: tabs opening in wrong profile when user switches Chrome profiles\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>\"\n```"
}

<sub>⏱️ 1482m 28s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Here's what's happening and the fix:

**Your problem:** When you switch Chrome profiles, CDP's `Target.createTarget` follows Chrome's active focus — so agent tabs open in YOUR current profile instead of theirs.

**The fix (being implemented now):**
1. **Profile-sticky sessions** — when an agent first touches a tab, we capture that tab's `browserContextId` and pin it to the session
2. **Auto-use pinned profile** — every subsequent `tabs.new` automatically passes the pinned `browserContextId` to `Target.createTarget`
3. **Works transparently** — agents don't need to do anything differently. Switch profiles all you want, agent tabs stay in their profile.
4. **Override available** — `tabs.new({ profile: "Work" })` or `tabs({ action: "set_profile", profile: "Personal" })` for explicit control

Both `server.js` (legacy) and `src/` (v5) are being updated. Waiting for the agent to finish.

**Profile-sticky sessions** — prevents agent tabs from drifting to the wrong Chrome profile:

   - `handleList` shows `[profile:...]` tags
   - Updated tool description documenting the behavior
4. **`server.js`** — Same fixes in legacy: session creation includes `browserContextId: null`, auto-detection on tab claim, profile-sticky `handleTabsNew`
5. **Tests** — 4 new unit tests covering profile pinning lifecycle

✅ `tsc --noEmit` passes | ✅ 146/146 tests pass | ✅ `node --check server.js` passes

## ✅ Profile-Sticky Sessions — Done

**The fix:** Sessions now automatically pin to the Chrome profile where they first interact. New tabs always open in the pinned profile, even when you manually switch profiles in Chrome.

| What | How |
|------|-----|
| Auto-detect | First tab claim captures `browserContextId` → pins to session |
| Auto-use | `tabs.new` passes pinned `browserContextId` to `Target.createTarget` |
| Explicit override | `tabs.new({ profile: "Work" })` or `tabs({ action: "set_profile" })` |
| Visible | `tabs.list` shows `[profile:...]` tags |
| Both versions | server.js (legacy) AND src/ (v5) updated |
| Tests | 146 passing (4 new profile tests) |

<sub>⏱️ 1493m 10s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot



Yes, exactly right. With the profile-sticky sessions we just implemented, here's how it works:

**Scenario:** Agent is applying for jobs using Tushar's profile (with Gmail, LinkedIn, etc. logged in)

1. Agent calls `tabs.new({ url: "https://linkedin.com" })` — first tab interaction
2. System auto-detects `browserContextId` from that tab → **pins session to Tushar's profile**
3. You switch to your personal profile to browse Reddit or whatever
4. Agent calls `tabs.new({ url: "https://greenhouse.io/apply" })` — system automatically passes the **pinned** `browserContextId`
5. Tab opens in **Tushar's profile** (not your personal one) ✅

The agent never needs to think about profiles — it just stays in whichever profile it started in. Your auth, cookies, LinkedIn login, Gmail session — all preserved in the right profile.

If the agent ever needs to explicitly switch (e.g., check a different Gmail account), it can use `tabs({ action: "set_profile", profile: "Personal" })`.

This is already committed and pushed to `dev` branch. Want me to merge `dev` → `main` so it's live?

New tabs always open in pinned profile even when user manually switches profiles\n- **`tabs.set_profile` action** — Explicitly switch a session's pinned Chrome profile\n- **Snapshot diffing** — `page.snapshot({ diff: true })` returns only changes since last snapshot on the tab\n- **Chrome extension scaffold** — `extension/` directory with Manifest V3, service worker, popup UI (bridge not yet implemented)\n- **macOS/Linux browser discovery** — Cross-platform paths for Chrome, Edge, Brave, Chromium\n\n### Fixed\n- Modal event listener leak on reconnect (register once, reset on disconnect)\n- JSON.parse crash on malformed CDP frames (try-catch guard)\n- Snapshot cache now wired into page.snapshot (store, invalidate, diff)\n\n### Changed\n- Version bumped to 5.0.0-alpha.2\n- Tests: 142 → 146 (profile pinning tests added)\n```\n\n## Step 2: Update README.md\n\nFind these sections and update:\n\n1. **Version badge**: Change `5.0.0-alpha.1` → `5.0.0-alpha.2`\n2. **Features section**: Add entries for:\n   - Cross-origin iframe support (OOP iframes visible in snapshots)\n   - File chooser interception (popup uploads, Google Picker)\n   - Profile-sticky sessions (tabs stay in the right profile)\n   - Cross-platform support (macOS, Linux, Windows)\n   - Chrome extension (scaffold, coming in v5.1)\n3. **FAQ section**: If there's a profile question, update it to mention auto-pinning\n\n## Step 3: Update package.json version\n\nIn `MCP Server/package.json`, change version from `5.0.0-alpha.1` to `5.0.0-alpha.2`.\n\n## Step 4: Update .github/copilot-instructions.md\n\nUpdate the version line at the top to `5.0.0-alpha.2`.\n\n## Step 5: Commit docs on dev\n\n```\ngit add -A\ngit commit -m \"docs: update for v5.0.0-alpha.2 — iframe, file chooser, profile sessions\n\n- CHANGELOG: v5.0.0-alpha.2 with iframe, file chooser, profile-sticky sessions\n- README: updated badges, features, FAQ\n- package.json: version bumped to 5.0.0-alpha.2\n- copilot-instructions: version updated\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>\"\n```\n\n## Step 6: Build to verify version\n```\ncd \"MCP Server\" && npx tsc\n```\n\n## Step 7: Merge dev → main\n```\ncd \"E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\"\ngit checkout main\ngit merge --no-ff dev -m \"Merge dev → main: v5.0.0-alpha.2\n\n- Cross-origin iframe support (OOP frames in snapshots)\n- File chooser interception (popup uploads, Google Picker/Dropbox)\n- CDP event pipeline (console, network, dialog, debugger routing)\n- Profile-sticky sessions (tabs stay in correct Chrome profile)\n- Snapshot diffing (incremental snapshots)\n- Chrome extension scaffold\n- macOS/Linux browser discovery\n- Bug fixes: listener leak, JSON.parse guard, snapshot cache wiring\n\n146 tests passing. Build clean.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>\"\n```\n\n## Step 8: Sync ALL branches to remote\n```\ngit push origin main\ngit push origin dev\ngit push origin backup/main-v4.13.0-pre-v5\n```\n\n## Step 9: Verify final state\n```\ngit --no-pager log --oneline main -n 5\ngit --no-pager branch -a\n```\n\nWhen done:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id IN ('update-docs-final', 'merge-dev-main')\n```"

<sub>⏱️ 31566m 3s</sub>

### ✅ `task`

**Diagnose + slim schemas + snapshots**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Diagnose + slim schemas + snapshots",
  "mode": "background",
  "name": "diagnose-and-slim",
  "prompt": "Three critical tasks: diagnose real failures from chat logs, then slim down tool schemas and snapshots for small models.\n\nWorking directory: `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server`\nBranch: `dev`\n\n## Task 1: Diagnose failures from chat2.md and chat3.md\n\nRead these files (they're large — read key sections):\n- `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\.temp\\chat2.md` — lines 1-200, then search for \"error\", \"fail\", \"connect\", \"LinkedIn\", \"+91\", \"country\", \"dropdown\"\n- `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\.temp\\chat3.md` — lines 1-50 (user complains about auto-connect), then search for same patterns\n\nCreate `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\.research\\DiagnosisReport.md` with EVERY specific failure pattern found. Organize by category.\n\n## Task 2: Slim tool schemas for 32K context models\n\nThe problem: 15 tools × complex schemas = massive ListTools response that overwhelms small models. \n\n### Solution A: Add a \"slim\" mode\n\nCreate `src/tools/slim-mode.ts`:\n\n```typescript\n/**\n * Slim mode reduces the tool surface for small-context models.\n * Instead of 15 tools with 90+ actions, expose 6 essential tools\n * with simplified schemas.\n * \n * Enable via: CDP_SLIM_MODE=true or { mode: \"slim\" } in config\n */\n\nexport interface SlimToolDef {\n  name: string;\n  description: string;\n  inputSchema: object;\n}\n\nexport function getSlimTools(): SlimToolDef[] {\n  return [\n    {\n      name: 'browser_navigate',\n      description: 'Navigate to URL. Auto-connects to browser.',\n      inputSchema: {\n        type: 'object',\n        properties: {\n          url: { type: 'string', description: 'URL to navigate to' },\n          tabId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tab ID (optional — uses active tab if omitted)' },\n        },\n        required: ['url'],\n      },\n    },\n    {\n      name: 'browser_snapshot',\n      description: 'Get page accessibility snapshot with element refs. Use refs to interact with elements.',\n      inputSchema: {\n        type: 'object',\n        properties: {\n          tabId: { type: 'string' },\n          maxLength: { type: 'number', description: 'Max chars to return (default: 8000)' },\n          interactive: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Only show interactive elements (buttons, inputs, links)' },\n        },\n      },\n    },\n    {\n      name: 'browser_click',\n      description: 'Click an element by ref from snapshot.',\n      inputSchema: {\n        type: 'object',\n        properties: {\n          ref: { type: 'number', description: 'Element ref from snapshot' },\n          tabId: { type: 'string' },\n        },\n        required: ['ref'],\n      },\n    },\n    {\n      name: 'browser_type',\n      description: 'Type text into focused element or element by ref.',\n      inputSchema: {\n        type: 'object',\n        properties: {\n          ref: { type: 'number', description: 'Element ref (optional — types into focused element)' },\n          text: { type: 'string', description: 'Text to type' },\n          tabId: { type: 'string' },\n          submit: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Press Enter after typing' },\n        },\n        required: ['text'],\n      },\n    },\n    {\n      name: 'browser_fill_form',\n      description: 'Fill multiple form fields at once. Handles text, dropdowns, checkboxes, all types.',\n      inputSchema: {\n        type: 'object',\n        properties: {\n          fields: {\n            type: 'array',\n            items: {\n              type: 'object',\n              properties: {\n                ref: { type: 'number', description: 'Element ref from snapshot' },\n                value: { type: 'string', description: 'Value to fill' },\n              },\n              required: ['ref', 'value'],\n            },\n          },\n          tabId: { type: 'string' },\n        },\n        required: ['fields'],\n      },\n    },\n    {\n      name: 'browser_tabs',\n      description: 'List open tabs, create new tab, or close a tab.',\n      inputSchema: {\n        type: 'object',\n        properties: {\n          action: { type: 'string', enum: ['list', 'new', 'close'], description: 'list: show tabs, new: open tab, close: close tab' },\n          url: { type: 'string', description: 'URL for new tab' },\n          tabId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tab to close' },\n        },\n      },\n    },\n  ];\n}\n\n// Maps slim tool calls to full tool calls\nexport function mapSlimToFull(slimName: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): { tool: string; args: Record<string, unknown> } {\n  switch (slimName) {\n    case 'browser_navigate':\n      return { tool: 'page', args: { action: 'goto', url: args.url, tabId: args.tabId } };\n    case 'browser_snapshot':\n      return { tool: 'page', args: { action: 'snapshot', tabId: args.tabId, maxLength: args.maxLength, interactive: args.interactive } };\n    case 'browser_click':\n      return { tool: 'interact', args: { action: 'click', uid: args.ref, tabId: args.tabId } };\n    case 'browser_type':\n      return { tool: 'interact', args: { action: 'type', uid: args.ref, text: args.text, tabId: args.tabId, submit: args.submit } };\n    case 'browser_fill_form':\n      return { tool: 'form', args: { action: 'fill', fields: (args.fields as any[])?.map(f => ({ uid: f.ref, value: f.value })), tabId: args.tabId } };\n    case 'browser_tabs':\n      if (args.action === 'new') return { tool: 'tabs', args: { action: 'new', url: args.url } };\n      if (args.action === 'close') return { tool: 'tabs', args: { action: 'close', tabId: args.tabId } };\n      return { tool: 'tabs', args: { action: 'list', showAll: true } };\n    default:\n      return { tool: slimName, args };\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Solution B: Add Playwright-compatible tool names as aliases\n\nIn `src/index.ts`, when `CDP_SLIM_MODE=true`:\n- Register only the 6 slim tools instead of the full 15\n- Use `mapSlimToFull` to route calls to the existing handlers\n- This way small models see 6 simple tools with tiny schemas\n\nAdd to `src/config.ts`:\n```typescript\nslimMode: process.env.CDP_SLIM_MODE === 'true',\n```\n\nWire in `src/index.ts`:\n```typescript\nif (config.slimMode) {\n  const slimTools = getSlimTools();\n  for (const tool of slimTools) {\n    registry.register(defineTool({\n      name: tool.name,\n      description: tool.description,\n      inputSchema: tool.inputSchema,\n      handler: async (ctx, args) => {\n        const mapped = mapSlimToFull(tool.name, args);\n        return registry.dispatch(mapped.tool, mapped.args, ctx);\n      },\n    }));\n  }\n} else {\n  // Register full tool set\n  registerTabsTools(registry, ctx);\n  // ... etc\n}\n```\n\n## Task 3: Optimize snapshots for small contexts\n\nIn `src/snapshot/token-optimizer.ts`, add new optimization modes:\n\n```typescript\n// New: interactive-only mode — only return elements users can interact with\nstatic interactiveOnly(nodes: AXNode[]): AXNode[] {\n  const INTERACTIVE_ROLES = new Set([\n    'button', 'link', 'textbox', 'combobox', 'checkbox', 'radio',\n    'slider', 'spinbutton', 'switch', 'tab', 'menuitem', 'option',\n    'searchbox', 'textarea', 'listbox', 'menu', 'menubar',\n    'treeitem', 'gridcell', 'row',\n  ]);\n  return filterTree(nodes, node => \n    INTERACTIVE_ROLES.has(node.role) || \n    node.role === 'heading' || // Keep headings for context\n    (node.role === 'img' && node.name) // Keep named images\n  );\n}\n\n// New: search mode — only return elements matching a query\nstatic search(nodes: AXNode[], query: string): AXNode[] {\n  const q = query.toLowerCase();\n  return filterTree(nodes, node =>\n    node.name?.toLowerCase().includes(q) ||\n    node.value?.toLowerCase().includes(q) ||\n    node.description?.toLowerCase().includes(q)\n  );\n}\n\n// New: max-length cap with smart truncation\nstatic capLength(serialized: string, maxChars: number): string {\n  if (serialized.length <= maxChars) return serialized;\n  // Find a good truncation point (end of a complete element)\n  const truncPoint = serialized.lastIndexOf('\\n', maxChars - 200);\n  return serialized.substring(0, truncPoint) + \n    `\\n\\n... (truncated ${serialized.length - truncPoint} chars. Use search parameter to find specific elements)`;\n}\n```\n\nUpdate `src/tools/page.ts` snapshot handler to support new params:\n- `interactive: boolean` — only return interactive elements\n- `search: string` — filter snapshot to matching elements  \n- `maxLength: number` — cap output size (default: 20000, slim mode: 8000)\n\nVerify: `npx tsc --noEmit`\n\nWhen done:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id IN ('diagnose-issues', 'slim-tool-schemas', 'slim-snapshots')\n```"
}
````

**Fix connection + forms + Playwright parity**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Fix connection + forms + Playwright parity",
  "mode": "background",
  "name": "fix-connection-forms-parity",
  "prompt": "Fix the three most impactful user-facing issues: connection reliability, form filling for real sites, and Playwright parity.\n\nWorking directory: `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\MCP Server`\nBranch: `dev`\n\n## Fix 1: Bulletproof Connection UX\n\nRead `src/connection/health-monitor.ts` and `src/index.ts`.\n\nThe auto-connect STILL fails and agents hallucinate. Make it truly zero-thought:\n\n### Changes to `src/connection/health-monitor.ts`:\n\n```typescript\nasync autoConnect(): Promise<void> {\n  if (this.client.isConnected) return;\n  if (this.connectPromise) return this.connectPromise;\n  \n  this.connectPromise = this._doAutoConnect();\n  try { await this.connectPromise; }\n  finally { this.connectPromise = null; }\n}\n\nprivate async _doAutoConnect(): Promise<void> {\n  // Strategy 1: Try saved connection (last known good)\n  if (this.lastGoodWsUrl) {\n    try {\n      await this.client.connect(this.lastGoodWsUrl);\n      this.onConnected();\n      return;\n    } catch { /* try next */ }\n  }\n  \n  // Strategy 2: Probe common ports via HTTP /json/version\n  const COMMON_PORTS = [9222, 9229, 9333, 9515];\n  for (const port of COMMON_PORTS) {\n    try {\n      const resp = await fetch(`http://127.0.0.1:${port}/json/version`, { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(2000) });\n      const data = await resp.json();\n      if (data.webSocketDebuggerUrl) {\n        await this.client.connect(data.webSocketDebuggerUrl);\n        this.lastGoodWsUrl = data.webSocketDebuggerUrl;\n        this.onConnected();\n        return;\n      }\n    } catch { continue; }\n  }\n  \n  // Strategy 3: File-based discovery (DevToolsActivePort)\n  const instances = discoverBrowserInstances();\n  if (instances.length > 0) {\n    const best = findBestInstance(instances, this.config.autoConnectProfile);\n    if (best) {\n      try {\n        await this.client.connect(best.wsUrl);\n        this.lastGoodWsUrl = best.wsUrl;\n        this.onConnected();\n        return;\n      } catch { /* fall through */ }\n    }\n  }\n  \n  // All strategies failed — throw CLEAR error\n  throw new ActionableError(\n    'Could not find any browser with remote debugging enabled.',\n    `To fix, do ONE of these:\\n` +\n    `  1. Chrome/Edge: Go to chrome://flags → search \"remote debugging\" → Enable → Relaunch\\n` +\n    `  2. Or launch with flag: chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222\\n` +\n    `  3. Or install the CDP Browser MCP extension (coming soon)\\n\\n` +\n    `The server will auto-connect once debugging is enabled. No manual connect needed.`\n  );\n}\n```\n\n### Changes to `src/index.ts`:\n\nMake the error message even clearer — when auto-connect fails, the tool response should say EXACTLY what to do:\n\n```typescript\n// In CallTool handler, when ensureConnected fails:\nif (!CONNECT_EXEMPT_TOOLS.has(name)) {\n  try {\n    await ensureConnected();\n  } catch (err) {\n    const ae = err instanceof ActionableError ? err : wrapError(err);\n    return {\n      content: [{\n        type: 'text',\n        text: `## Browser Not Connected\\n\\n${ae.message}\\n\\n### How to fix\\n${ae.fix}\\n\\n` +\n              `After fixing, just retry your command — no reconnect step needed.`\n      }],\n      isError: true,\n    };\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Fix 2: Real-World Form Filling\n\nThe form engine fails on real sites because:\n1. **Country code dropdowns (+91)**: These are custom comboboxes, not native `<select>`. The combobox handler types but doesn't wait long enough for async search.\n2. **LinkedIn Easy Apply location**: The location field is an autocomplete that needs debounce waiting.\n3. **Typing too fast**: React inputs drop characters when typed too fast.\n\n### Changes to `src/tools/form.ts`:\n\n**Fix character-by-character typing speed:**\n```typescript\n// In fillText — increase inter-char delay for React compatibility\nconst CHAR_DELAY = 30; // ms between characters (was 10-20)\n```\n\n**Fix combobox — longer wait, better matching:**\n```typescript\n// In fillCombobox:\n// 1. Type slower (80ms between chars instead of 50ms)\n// 2. Wait longer for options (5s instead of 3s)  \n// 3. After typing full value, wait 1s for debounced APIs\n// 4. If no exact match, try with just first 3 chars (for autocomplete fields)\n```\n\n**Add phone country code handler:**\n```typescript\n// Detect phone country code fields: combobox near a phone input, or combobox with +1/+91 type options\n// Strategy: type the country name OR the dial code, wait for dropdown, select\nasync function fillCountryCode(ctx, sessionId, uid, value) {\n  // value could be \"+91\" or \"India\" or \"IN\"\n  // Normalize: if starts with +, search for the number\n  // If it's a country name, search for the name\n  // If it's a 2-letter code, map to country name first\n}\n```\n\n**Add location autocomplete handler:**\n```typescript  \n// For location/address autocomplete fields:\n// 1. Type the value slowly (100ms per char)\n// 2. Wait 2s for suggestions to load (they hit an API)\n// 3. Look for listbox/menu that appeared\n// 4. Select first matching suggestion\n// 5. If no suggestion, press Enter to accept typed value\n```\n\n### Changes to `src/tools/interact.ts`:\n\n**Fix typing for React inputs:**\n```typescript\n// In handleType — use Input.insertText for bulk text, \n// then dispatch 'input' and 'change' events via Runtime.evaluate\n// This is how Playwright does it — much more reliable than char-by-char keyDown/keyUp\n```\n\n## Fix 3: Playwright Parity\n\nRead `E:\\Projects\\CDP Browser Automation\\.research\\PlaywrightMCP.md` sections 2, 4, 5, 6.\n\nKey patterns to adopt:\n\n1. **waitForCompletion after EVERY click/type/fill** — Playwright wraps every action in `waitForCompletion` which tracks network requests and waits for them to settle. Implement this:\n\nIn `src/tools/interact.ts`, wrap click/type handlers:\n```typescript\n// After every click that might trigger navigation or API calls:\n// Wait for network to settle (no new requests for 500ms)\n```\n\n2. **Use `ref` not `uid` in user-facing output** — Playwright uses `ref` consistently. Our tool uses `uid` in some places, `ref` in others. Standardize to `ref` in all snapshots and tool params.\n\nActually, keep `uid` internally but alias `ref` in the slim mode tools.\n\n3. **Trim tool descriptions** — Playwright's descriptions are 1-3 lines. Our descriptions are paragraphs. For the full-mode tools, trim descriptions to be more concise while keeping the essential guidance.\n\n## After all fixes:\n- `npx tsc --noEmit` must pass\n- `npx vitest run` must pass\n\nCommit:\n```\ngit add -A\ngit commit -m \"feat: bulletproof connection, real-world form filling, slim mode, Playwright parity\n\nConnection:\n- Multi-strategy auto-connect: saved URL → port probe → file discovery\n- Crystal clear error messages with exact fix steps\n- lastGoodWsUrl caching for instant reconnect\n\nForm filling:\n- Country code dropdown handler (+91, India, IN all work)\n- Location autocomplete handler (slow type + API wait)\n- Slower character typing for React compatibility (30-80ms)\n- Longer combobox wait (5s for async options)\n\nSlim mode (CDP_SLIM_MODE=true):\n- 6 Playwright-compatible tools instead of 15\n- Tiny schemas for 32K context models\n- Maps to full tool handlers internally\n\nSnapshots:\n- interactive-only mode (buttons, inputs, links only)\n- search parameter (find specific elements)\n- maxLength cap with smart truncation\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>\"\n```\n\nWhen done:\n```sql\nUPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id IN ('fix-connection-ux', 'fix-form-filling', 'playwright-parity')\n```"
}
````

All three fixes committed. Here's a summary:

**Fix 1 — Connection UX** (`health-monitor.ts`):
- 3-strategy auto-connect: cached URL → port probing (9222/9229/9333/9515) → DevToolsActivePort file discovery
- `lastGoodWsUrl` caching for instant reconnect
- Actionable error with exact fix steps when all strategies fail

**Fix 2 — Form Filling** (`form.ts`):
- Combobox: 80ms char delay (was 50), 1s debounce wait, 5s poll timeout (was 3s)
- Short-prefix retry: re-types first 3 chars when full value yields no results
- New `country-code` handler: normalizes +91/India/IN → combobox search
- New `location` handler: 100ms/char typing + 2s API wait + suggestion selection

**Fix 3 — Playwright Parity** (`interact.ts` + `form.ts`):
- `waitForNetworkSettle()` after click actions — waits for pending XHR/fetch
- Trimmed `INTERACT_DESCRIPTION` from 32→19 lines, `FORM_DESCRIPTION` from 30→11 lines

✅ `tsc --noEmit` passes, ✅ 146/146 tests pass.

| Feature | Problem it solves |

| **Interactive snapshots** | 34KB snapshots → only buttons/inputs/links |
| **Snapshot search** | `search: "Login"` → only matching elements |
| **Snapshot maxLength** | Smart truncation at line boundaries |
| **3-strategy auto-connect** | Cached URL → port probe → file discovery |
| **Country code handler** | +91, India, IN all work in phone dropdowns |
| **Location autocomplete** | Slow type + API debounce wait |
| **Combobox timing fix** | 80ms delay, 5s poll, short-prefix retry |
| **Network settle** | Playwright-style wait after clicks |
| **185 tests** | +39 new tests for all new features |
| **Diagnosis report** | 15 failure patterns documented |

<details>

```
1. /**
2.  * CDPClient — typed WebSocket client for the Chrome DevTools Protocol.
3.  *
4.  * Extracted from the monolithic server.js.  Provides:
5.  *  • Promise-based `send(method, params?, sessionId?)` with auto-timeout
6.  *  • Internal message-ID tracking and callback resolution
7.  *  • Typed EventEmitter for CDP domain events
8.  *  • Connection-state accessors
9.  *  • Session pooling: reuses CDP sessions for already-attached targets
10.  *  • Target caching: avoids redundant Target.getTargets() HTTP calls
11.  */
12. 
13. import { EventEmitter } from 'events';
14. import WebSocket from 'ws';
15. import type { CDPMessage, CDPEvent, CDPResponse } from '../types.js';
16. 
17. // ─── Types ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18. 
19. export type ConnectionState = 'disconnected' | 'connecting' | 'connected';
20. 
21. export interface CDPClientOptions {
22.   /** Per-command timeout in ms (default 30 000). */
23.   commandTimeout?: number;
24.   /** How long (ms) to cache Target.getTargets() results (default 2000). */
25.   targetCacheTTL?: number;
26. }
27. 
28. interface PendingCallback {
29.   resolve: (value: unknown) => void;
30.   reject: (reason: Error) => void;
31. }
32. 
33. /** Cached target list result */
34. interface TargetCache {
35.   targets: unknown;
36.   timestamp: number;
37. }
38. 
39. // Re-export protocol message types for convenience
40. export type { CDPMessage, CDPEvent, CDPResponse };
41. 
42. // ─── CDPClient ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43. 
44. /**
45.  * Events emitted by CDPClient:
46.  *
47.  *  • `'event'`        — every CDP event  `(event: CDPEvent) => void`
48.  *  • `'connected'`    — WebSocket opened
49.  *  • `'disconnected'` — WebSocket closed (possibly unexpectedly)
50.  *  • `'error'`        — connection-level error
51.  */
52. export class CDPClient extends EventEmitter {
53.   private ws: WebSocket | null = null;
54.   private messageId = 0;
55.   private callbacks = new Map<number, PendingCallback>();
56.   private _state: ConnectionState = 'disconnected';
57.   private defaultTimeout: number;
58. 
59.   // Session pooling: targetId → CDP sessionId (avoid re-attaching)
60.   private sessionPool = new Map<string, string>();
61. 
62.   // Target caching: avoid redundant Target.getTargets() calls
63.   private targetCache: TargetCache | null = null;
64.   private targetCacheTTL: number;
65. 
66.   constructor(opts: CDPClientOptions = {}) {
67.     super();
68.     this.defaultTimeout = opts.commandTimeout ?? 30_000;
69.     this.targetCacheTTL = opts.targetCacheTTL ?? 2_000;
70.   }
71. 
72.   // ── Accessors ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
73. 
74.   get state(): ConnectionState {
75.     return this._state;
76.   }
77. 
78.   get isConnected(): boolean {
79.     return this._state === 'connected' && this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN;
80.   }
81. 
82.   /** The underlying WebSocket URL (if connected). */
83.   get url(): string | null {
84.     return this.ws?.url ?? null;
85.   }
86. 
87.   /** Expose raw WebSocket for health-check ping/pong. */
88.   get rawSocket(): WebSocket | null {
89.     return this.ws;
90.   }
91. 
92.   // ── Connect / Disconnect ────────────────────────────────────────
93. 
94.   /**
95.    * Open a WebSocket connection to the given CDP endpoint.
96.    * Resolves once the socket is open; rejects on connection error.
97.    */
98.   async connect(wsUrl: string): Promise<void> {
99.     if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) return;
100. 
101.     this._state = 'connecting';
102. 
103.     return new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
104.       const socket = new WebSocket(wsUrl, { perMessageDeflate: false });
105. 
106.       socket.once('open', () => {
107.         this.ws = socket;
108.         this._state = 'connected';
109.         this._attachListeners(socket);
110.         this.emit('connected');
111.         resolve();
112.       });
113. 
114.       socket.once('error', (err: Error) => {
115.         this._state = 'disconnected';
116.         reject(
117.           new Error(
118.             'Cannot connect to browser. Enable remote debugging:\n' +
119.               'Chrome: chrome://flags → #enable-remote-debugging → Enabled → Relaunch\n' +
120.               'Edge: edge://flags → #enable-remote-debugging → Enabled → Relaunch\n' +
121.               'Brave: brave://flags → #enable-remote-debugging → Enabled → Relaunch\n' +
122.               `Or launch with --remote-debugging-port=9222\n(${err.message})`,
123.           ),
124.         );
125.       });
126.     });
127.   }
128. 
129.   /**
130.    * Gracefully close the WebSocket.
131.    * Returns a promise that resolves once the socket is fully closed.
132.    */
133.   async disconnect(): Promise<void> {
134.     const socket = this.ws;
135.     if (!socket || socket.readyState === WebSocket.CLOSED) {
136.       this._cleanup();
137.       return;
138.     }
139. 
140.     return new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
141.       socket.once('close', () => {
142.         // _cleanup is called by the 'close' listener already
143.         resolve();
144.       });
145.       socket.close();
146.     });
147.   }
148. 
149.   /** Forcefully terminate the WebSocket without a graceful close. */
150.   terminate(): void {
151.     if (this.ws) {
152.       try {
153.         this.ws.terminate();
154.       } catch {
155.         /* ok */
156.       }
157.     }
158.     this._cleanup();
159.   }
160. 
161.   // ── CDP Command ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
162. 
163.   /**
164.    * Send a CDP command and await its result.
165.    *
166.    * @param method    CDP method, e.g. `"Page.navigate"`
167.    * @param params    Method parameters (optional)
168.    * @param sessionId CDP session ID for per-tab commands (optional)
169.    * @param timeout   Override the default command timeout (optional)
170.    */
171.   async send(
172.     method: string,
173.     params: Record<string, unknown> = {},
174.     sessionId?: string,
175.     timeout?: number,
176.   ): Promise<unknown> {
177.     if (!this.ws || this.ws.readyState !== WebSocket.OPEN) {
178.       throw new Error(`CDPClient: not connected (state=${this._state})`);
179.     }
180. 
181.     const id = ++this.messageId;
182.     const timeoutMs = timeout ?? this.defaultTimeout;
183. 
184.     return new Promise<unknown>((resolve, reject) => {
185.       const timer = setTimeout(() => {
186.         this.callbacks.delete(id);
187.         reject(new Error(`${method}: timed out (${timeoutMs}ms)`));
188.       }, timeoutMs);
189. 
190.       this.callbacks.set(id, {
191.         resolve(value: unknown) {
192.           clearTimeout(timer);
193.           resolve(value);
194.         },
195.         reject(err: Error) {
196.           clearTimeout(timer);
197.           reject(err);
198.         },
199.       });
200. 
201.       const msg: CDPMessage = { id, method, params };
202.       if (sessionId) msg.sessionId = sessionId;
203.       this.ws!.send(JSON.stringify(msg));
204.     });
205.   }
206. 
207.   // ── Internal ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
208. 
209.   private _attachListeners(socket: WebSocket): void {
210.     socket.on('message', (raw: WebSocket.RawData) => {
211.       let data: CDPResponse | CDPEvent;
212.       try {
213.         data = JSON.parse(raw.toString()) as CDPResponse | CDPEvent;
214.       } catch (err) {
215.         this.emit('error', new Error(`Malformed CDP frame: ${raw.toString().substring(0, 200)}`));
216.         return; // Don't crash — skip malformed frame
217.       }
218. 
219.       // Response to a command we sent
220.       if ('id' in data && data.id !== undefined) {
221.         const resp = data as CDPResponse;
222.         const cb = this.callbacks.get(resp.id);
223.         if (cb) {
224.           this.callbacks.delete(resp.id);
225.           if (resp.error) {
226.             cb.reject(new Error(resp.error.message));
227.           } else {
228.             cb.resolve(resp.result);
229.           }
230.         }
231.         return;
232.       }
233. 
234.       // CDP event (domain notification)
235.       const event = data as CDPEvent;
236.       if (event.method) {
237.         this.emit('event', event);
238.       }
239.     });
240. 
241.     socket.on('close', () => {
242.       this._cleanup();
243.       this.emit('disconnected');
244.     });
245. 
246.     socket.on('error', (err: Error) => {
247.       this.emit('error', err);
248.     });
249.   }
250. 
251.   /**
252.    * Reject all pending callbacks and reset internal state.
253.    * Called on WebSocket close (intentional or not).
254.    */
255.   private _cleanup(): void {
256.     for (const { reject } of this.callbacks.values()) {
257.       reject(new Error('WebSocket closed'));
258.     }
259.     this.callbacks.clear();
260.     this.sessionPool.clear();
261.     this.targetCache = null;
262.     this.ws = null;
263.     this._state = 'disconnected';
264.   }
265. 
266.   // ── Session Pooling ────────────────────────────────────────────
267. 
268.   /**
269.    * Get a cached CDP session ID for a target, or null if not pooled.
270.    * Avoids redundant Target.attachToTarget calls for already-attached targets.
271.    */
272.   getPooledSession(targetId: string): string | null {
273.     return this.sessionPool.get(targetId) ?? null;
274.   }
275. 
276.   /**
277.    * Store a CDP session ID for a target in the pool.
278.    */
279.   poolSession(targetId: string, sessionId: string): void {
280.     this.sessionPool.set(targetId, sessionId);
281.   }
282. 
283.   /**
284.    * Remove a session from the pool (e.g. on detach or target close).
285.    */
286.   unpoolSession(targetId: string): void {
287.     this.sessionPool.delete(targetId);
288.   }
289. 
290.   /** Number of pooled sessions */
291.   get pooledSessionCount(): number {
292.     return this.sessionPool.size;
293.   }
294. 
295.   // ── Target Caching ─────────────────────────────────────────────
296. 
297.   /**
298.    * Get targets with caching. If a valid cached result exists within
299.    * the TTL window, returns it without issuing a CDP command.
300.    * Otherwise fetches fresh targets and caches the result.
301.    */
302.   async getTargetsCached(): Promise<unknown> {
303.     const now = Date.now();
304.     if (
305.       this.targetCache &&
306.       now - this.targetCache.timestamp < this.targetCacheTTL
307.     ) {
308.       return this.targetCache.targets;
309.     }
310. 
311.     const result = await this.send('Target.getTargets');
312.     this.targetCache = { targets: result, timestamp: Date.now() };
313.     return result;
314.   }
315. 
316.   /**
317.    * Invalidate the target cache (e.g. after creating/closing a tab).
318.    */
319.   invalidateTargetCache(): void {
320.     this.targetCache = null;
321.   }
322. }
323.
```

</details>
